steps.description: Melt butter and semi-sweet chocolate together in a medium saucepan on medium heat, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, about 15 minutes. You can place the melted chocolate/butter mixture into the refrigerator for a few minutes to speed this up, if desired. Don’t refrigerate too long, as the butter will start to re-solidify.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Whisk sugar into cooled chocolate/butter mixture. Add in the eggs one at a time, whisking after each addition until combined. Whisk in the vanilla, and then gently fold in the flour and salt. Pour batter into prepared pan, reserving about 1/4 cup of batter (doesn’t have to be exact).

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Using a handheld mixer or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the cream cheese on medium-high speed in a large bowl until smooth and creamy, about 1 minute. Beat in the sugar, egg yolk, peppermint extract, and 2 drops of food coloring until completely smooth, about 1 minute. Feel free to add more green food coloring, if desired. Fold in the mini chocolate chips.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Dollop spoonfuls of the cream cheese mixture on top of the prepared brownie batter. Spoon remaining 1/4 cup of brownie batter on top. Glide a knife through the layers, creating a swirl pattern. Add more mini chocolate chips on top, if desired.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Bake brownies for 30–35 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out mostly clean with just a few moist crumbs. I recommend tenting the brownies with aluminum foil after about 20 minutes to protect the top from over-browning. Remove from the oven and place the pan on a wire rack. Cool brownies completely.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Lift the brownies out using the parchment paper overhang on the sides. Cut into squares.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Cover leftover brownies and store at room temperature for up to 1 day or in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

title: Creamy Vegetable Wild Rice Soup
description: This ultra-creamy Vegetable Wild Rice Soup is chock-full of delicious vegetables and a wild rice blend, with no dairy or other animal ingredients.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Wash and slice the mushrooms. Add the olive oil and mushrooms to a large soup pot and sauté over medium heat until the mushrooms are fully wilted and browned on the edges (cook until all the moisture is released, evaporated, and the mushrooms begin to actually brown for the best flavor).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: While the mushrooms are sautéing, mince the garlic, dice the onion, slice the celery, and peel and slice the carrots.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add the garlic, onion, celery, carrots, thyme, and sage to the pot with the mushrooms. Continue to sauté for about five minutes more, or until the onions are soft.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Finally, add the wild rice blend and vegetable broth, and stir to combine. Place a lid on the pot, turn the heat up to medium-high, and bring it to a boil. Once boiling, turn the heat down and let the soup simmer for about 45 minutes with the lid in place (or however long the instructions on your brand of rice suggests for cooking).

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Once the soup has simmered and the rice is tender, add the coconut milk. Stir to combine and allow it to simmer for about 5 minutes more. Finally, taste the soup and add salt if needed (I did not add any, but this will depend on the salt content of your broth). Serve hot with crusty bread for dipping!

title: Very Vanilla Cupcakes
description: My favorite homemade vanilla cupcake recipe. Ditch that boxed mix-- these taste way better!

ingredients.name: (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
seeds scraped from 1/2 split vanilla bean*
(16 Tbsp; 226g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
seeds scraped from 1/2 split vanilla bean*

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 350°F (177°C). Line 12-count muffin pan with cupcake liners. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a medium bowl, mix together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside. In a large microwave-safe bowl, melt butter in the microwave. Whisk in sugar—mixture will be gritty. Whisk in egg whites, yogurt, milk, and vanilla extract until combined. Split 1 vanilla bean down the middle lengthwise. Scrape seeds from half of the vanilla bean into batter. Reserve other half.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Slowly mix dry ingredients into the wet ingredients until no lumps remain. Batter will be thick.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Divide batter among 12 cupcake liners and bake for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow to cool.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: To make the frosting, beat softened butter on medium speed with an electric or stand mixer. Beat for about 3 minutes until smooth and creamy. Add confectioners’ sugar, cream, vanilla extract, and vanilla bean seeds with the mixer running. Increase to high speed and beat for 3 minutes. Add more powdered sugar if frosting is too thin or more cream if mixture is too thick. Add salt if frosting is too sweet (1/4 teaspoon). Frost cooled cupcakes (I used Wilton 1M piping tip). There may be leftover frosting depending how much you use on each cupcake.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Store cupcakes in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days and in the refrigerator up to 7 days. I recommend a cupcake carrier for storing and transporting decorated cupcakes.

title: Teriyaki Chicken (With Homemade Teriyaki Sauce)
description: Make your own Teriyaki Sauce at home - not only is it easy, fast and more tasty, the marinade infuses into the flesh better. Make a larger batch and store it in the fridge so you have it on hand for a quick meal!

ingredients.name: chicken thigh fillets (, skinless and boneless (about 5oz/150g each))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Teriyaki Sauce Marinade ("Marinade")

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Chicken

title: Easy Homemade Balsamic Vinaigrette
description: This super simple homemade balsamic vinaigrette is perfect for all your green salads and pasta salads and has several options for customizing!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Add all the ingredients to a jar, bowl, or blender. Close the jar and shake, whisk in the bowl, or pulse in a blender until the ingredients are combined and smooth.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Let the dressing stand 5-10 minutes for the flavors to blend, then serve or refrigerate for up to 5 days.

title: Raspberry Swirl Sweet Rolls
description: In this recipe, tender, buttery dough spirals around a juicy, sweet-tart raspberry filling made from frozen raspberries.

ingredients.name: and 1/2 Tablespoons (14g) Platinum Yeast from Red Star (2 standard-size packets)*
(113g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature and cut into 4 pieces
pure vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste
and 1/2 cups (563g) all-purpose flour or bread flour (spooned & leveled), plus more as needed
canola, vegetable, or olive oil for bowl (or use nonstick spray)
one 10-oz. package (about 2.5 cups or 300g) frozen raspberries (do not thaw)
(113g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature
pure vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Whisk the warm milk, 2 Tablespoons sugar, and the yeast together in the bowl of your stand mixer fitted with a dough hook or paddle attachment. Cover and allow mixture to sit for about 5 minutes or until foamy on top. *If you do not own a mixer, you can do this in a large mixing bowl and in the next step, mix the dough together with a large wooden spoon/silicone spatula. It will take a bit of arm muscle. A hand mixer works, but the sticky dough repeatedly gets stuck in the beaters. Mixing by hand with a wooden spoon or silicone spatula is a better choice.*

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: On medium speed, beat in the remaining sugar (which should be 1/2 cup/100g) and the softened butter until it is slightly broken up. Add the eggs, vanilla, and salt and beat on medium speed until combined. The butter won’t really be mixing into the mixture, so don’t be alarmed if it stays in pieces. Switch the mixer down to low speed and with it running, add 1 cup of flour at a time, making sure it’s fully incorporated before adding the next. After 4 cups have been added, add the last 1/2 cup and beat until the dough comes together and pulls away from the sides of the bowl, about 3 minutes. Dough will be soft.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Keep the dough in the mixer (and switch to the dough hook if you used the paddle) and beat for an additional 5 full minutes, or knead by hand on a lightly floured surface for 5 full minutes. (If you’re new to bread-baking, my How to Knead Dough video tutorial can help here.) If the dough becomes too sticky during the kneading process, sprinkle 1 teaspoon of flour at a time on the dough or on the work surface/in the bowl to make a soft, slightly tacky dough. Do not add more flour than you need because you do not want a dry dough. After kneading, the dough should still feel a little soft. Poke it with your finger—if it slowly bounces back, your dough is ready to rise. You can also do a “windowpane test” to see if your dough has been kneaded long enough: tear off a small (roughly golfball-size) piece of dough and gently stretch it out until it’s thin enough for light to pass through it. Hold it up to a window or light. Does light pass through the stretched dough without the dough tearing first? If so, your dough has been kneaded long enough and is ready to rise. If not, keep kneading until it passes the windowpane test.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Lightly grease a large bowl with oil or use nonstick spray. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it to coat all sides in the oil. Cover the bowl with aluminum foil, plastic wrap, or a clean kitchen towel. Allow the dough to rise in a relatively warm environment for 2 hours or until double in size. (I always let it rise on the counter and it takes about 2 hours. For a tiny reduction in rise time, see my answer to Where Should Dough Rise? in my Baking with Yeast Guide.)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Grease the bottom and sides of a metal or glass 9×13-inch baking dish or line with parchment paper.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Punch down the dough to release the air. Place dough on a lightly floured work surface and using a lightly floured rolling pin, roll dough into a 12×18-inch rectangle. Make sure the dough is smooth and evenly thick. If the dough keeps shrinking as you roll it out, stop what you’re doing, cover it lightly, and let it rest for 10 minutes to relax the gluten. When you return to the dough, it should stretch out much easier.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: In a medium bowl, toss the frozen raspberries with the sugar and cornstarch. Spread the cold sugared raspberries evenly over the dough. Tightly roll up the dough to form an 18-inch-long log. With an extra sharp knife, cut into 12 even rolls, about 1.5 inches each. Arrange in the prepared baking pan.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Cover the rolls tightly and allow to rise until puffy, about 90 minutes. (Or use the overnight option in the Notes below.) The berries will release their juice at the bottom of the pan—this is OK.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 350°F (177°C).

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Bake for about 28–32 minutes or until they are lightly browned on top. About halfway through baking time, tent a piece of aluminum foil over the pan to prevent the tops from browning too quickly. Remove pan from the oven and place pan on a wire rack for about 10 minutes as you make the icing.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: In a medium bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat the cream cheese on medium speed until smooth and creamy. Add the confectioners’ sugar, cream/milk, and vanilla. Beat on low speed for 30 seconds, then switch to high speed and beat for 1 minute. Spread the icing over the warm rolls and serve immediately.

steps.title: Step 12
steps.description: Cover leftover frosted or unfrosted rolls tightly and store at room temperature for up to 2 days or in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: Fall Apart Oven Baked Ribs with Chipotle BBQ Sauce
description: Pork ribs slow cooked in the oven until tender and falling off the bone, coated with a homemade Barbecue Sauce with a great balance of sweet, tang and heat. A one pan recipe!

ingredients.name: / 2.5 kg American style pork ribs (, cut into individual ribs (to fit into one baking dish))
bottle dark beer ((About 12 oz / 375 ml. Stout, porter or a dark ale))
/ 100g can Chipotle in Adobo Sauce (, chipotle and sauce (1/2 a standard can) (Note 1))
large onion ((brown, white or yellow), diced)
white wine vinegar ((or 2 tbsp normal white vinegar + 1 tsp sugar))
Tabasco sauce (, or other hot sauce. Adjust to taste.)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Combine the Rub ingredients in a bowl.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Pat the ribs dry, then rub with the Rub. If time permits, refrigerate, covered, for at least 2 hours or overnight.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Take ribs out of the fridge, place in a large baking dish in a single layer (squished is ok, they will shrink slightly).

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Preheat oven to 160C/325F.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Pour beer into the baking dish and cover with a lid (use foil if you don't have a lid). Place in the oven and cook for 1 hour.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Meanwhile, make the Sauce.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Take out of the oven and remove lid. Pour the sauce over the ribs, then return to the oven (uncovered).

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Bake for 45 minutes to 1 hour 15 minutes, turning once or twice, until the ribs are cooked to your liking. The meat should be very tender and comes off the bone easily, and the sauce should be thick and "jam-like". If the pan starts looking dry, add a splash of water.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: To finish the ribs off, grill/broil for 5 to 10 minutes to get a nice browned finish on top (this is an optional step).

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Serve, garnished with fresh coriander/cilantro leaves and wedges of lime.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Sauce

title: Roasted Vegetable Salad Meal Prep
description: These Roasted Vegetable Salad Meal Prep boxes are an easy, no-reheat, plan-ahead lunch option that will help you get your daily vegetables!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Make the dressing first. In a small bowl combine the garlic powder, onion powder, dill, parsley, salt, pepper, tahini, water, and lemon juice. Stir or whisk until smooth. Refrigerate the dressing as you prepare the rest of the salad.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Begin cooking the quinoa. Rinse the quinoa well in a fine wire mesh sieve. Place the rinsed quinoa in a small sauce pot and add 1.75 cups water. Place a lid on top, turn the heat on to high, and bring it up to a boil. Once it reaches a boil, turn the heat down to low and let it simmer for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: After letting the quinoa simmer for 15 minutes, turn the heat off and let it rest with the lid in place for five minutes. Empty the cooked quinoa out into a casserole dish, spreading it out to increase the surface area. Refrigerate the dish with the quinoa until you're ready to assemble the meal preps.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 400ºF. Place the grape tomatoes onto a large baking sheet. Slice the onion into 1/4-inch strips. Cut the zucchini and yellow squash into 1/4-inch wide half-rounds. Cut the mushrooms in half. Mix the vegetables together on the baking sheet. If there are too many to fit in a single layer on the baking sheet, divide them between two baking sheets.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add the olive oil, oregano, garlic powder, and salt to the vegetables. Use your hands to mix the vegetables and seasoning, until the vegetables are well coated in oil.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Roast the vegetables in the preheated 400ºF oven, stirring every 15 minutes, until the vegetables are browned on the edges. Allow the vegetables to cool for about 10 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: To build the meal prep bowls, place one heaping cup of spinach in each dish. Add 1/2 cup of the cooked and cooled quinoa on top of the spinach. Divide the roasted vegetables between the four containers, then add 2 Tbsp hummus to each dish.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Divide the dressing between four small dressing containers. Refrigerate the salads and dressing for up to 4 days.

title: Snickerdoodle Cupcakes with Cinnamon Swirl Frosting
description: Cinnamon-sugar-swirled vanilla cupcakes topped with creamy cinnamon-swirled frosting... these snickerdoodle cupcakes always get rave reviews. Try them and you'll taste why!

ingredients.name: (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
(60g) sour cream or plain yogurt, at room temperature*
(16 Tbsp; 226g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
optional for garnish: 12 cinnamon sticks or 12 mini snickerdoodles*

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 350°F (177°C). Line a 12-cup muffin pan with cupcake liners.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a large bowl, whisk the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together. Set aside. In a medium bowl, whisk the melted butter and 1 cup (200g) sugar together. Mixture will be gritty. Whisk in the egg, sour cream, milk, and vanilla extract until combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and whisk until the batter is completely combined. Batter should be creamy and mostly smooth; a few small lumps are OK.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: In a small bowl, mix the remaining 1/3 cup of sugar and 2 teaspoons cinnamon together. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Spoon 2 scant Tablespoons of cupcake batter into each cupcake liner. Top with 1 teaspoon of the cinnamon-sugar mixture. Top with 1 heaping Tablespoon of cupcake batter, until the cupcake liners are about 3/4 full. Some of the cinnamon-sugar filling from the center layer may peek through the top layer of batter—that’s ok. Top each cupcake with 1/4 teaspoon of cinnamon-sugar. Swirl each gently with a toothpick.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Bake for 20–22 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove cupcakes from the oven and allow them to cool in the pan for 10 minutes, and then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely. Shake off any excess cinnamon-sugar that isn’t sticking to the tops, as that will prevent the frosting from sticking to the cupcakes.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: With a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat butter on high speed until smooth and creamy, about 1 minute. Add confectioners’ sugar, heavy cream, and vanilla extract. Beat on low speed for 30 seconds, then switch to high speed and beat for 3 full minutes. Add more confectioners’ sugar if frosting seems thin and greasy or more cream if the frosting seems too stiff. Taste, and add salt if frosting is too sweet (I like to add about 1/8 teaspoon). Remove half of the frosting from the mixing bowl and transfer to another bowl, or directly into your piping bag. Beat 1 Tablespoon of cinnamon into the remaining frosting in the mixing bowl.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Spoon half cinnamon frosting into the piping bag and half vanilla frosting into the piping bag. See above for a visual. Pipe onto cupcakes. I used a Wilton 1M piping tip. Garnish cupcakes with cinnamon sticks or mini snickerdoodle cookies, if desired.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Cover and store leftover cupcakes at room temperature for up to 1 day or in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. I recommend a cupcake carrier for storing and transporting decorated cupcakes.

title: Crispy Potatoes with Lemon Dill Tahini Dressing
description: These Crispy Potatoes with Lemon Dill Tahini Dressing are like a deconstructed vegan potato salad, with a creamy homemade dressing.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Place the potatoes in a pot and add 1/4 tsp salt and enough water to cover the potatoes by one inch. Place a lid on the pot, turn the heat on to high, and allow the water to come to a boil. Boil the potatoes for 8-10 minutes, or until they are fork tender.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: While the potatoes are boiling, prepare the Lemon Dill Tahini Dressing. Add the garlic powder, onion powder, dried dill, dried parsley, salt, some freshly cracked pepper (about 10 cranks of a pepper mill), tahini, water, and lemon juice to a bowl. Stir or whisk until smooth. If the dressing is too runny, place it in the refrigerator and it will thicken as it chills.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Drain the potatoes and let them cool for 5 minutes, or until they are cool enough to handle. Once slightly cooled, slice the potatoes in half.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add 2 Tbsp cooking oil to a non-stick skillet (I prefer cast iron for this recipe). Heat the oil over medium-high. Once hot, add the potatoes. Let the potatoes cook, stirring only occasionally, until they are browned and crispy on the outside (about 10 minutes total).

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: While the potatoes are frying in the skillet, slice the green onions.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Remove the skillet from the heat and either transfer the potatoes to a serving dish or use the skillet as the serving dish. Drizzle a generous amount of the lemon dill tahini dressing over top, then add the green onions. Serve immediately.

title: Vegetarian Chow Mein
description: This vegetarian/vegan version of chow mein is very close to the traditional version, but I have created a sauce without oyster sauce in it - and obviously substituted the meat with tofu.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Prepare noodles according to packet instructions (usually soaking it in boiling water).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Combine tofu with marinade and set aside for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Combine sauce ingredients in a small bowl.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Heat oil in wok over high heat, add garlic and stir fry for 10 seconds.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add tofu and stir fry until starting to caramelise - about 1 minute. Stir fry gently so the tofu doesn't break.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Add the cabbage and carrot and stir fry until the cabbage starts to wilt.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Add the noodles, scallions/shallots, bean sprouts and sauce and stir fry until the noodles are warmed through and sauce evenly coats the noodles - about 1 minute. Don't cook it too much - the bean sprouts should still be quite crisp, not wilted.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Serve immediately.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Directions using homemade Real Chinese All Purpose Stir Fry Sauce

title: Lemon Dill Salmon and Kale Salad
description: This super simple Lemon Dill Salmon and Kale Salad is a fast, convenient, and filling lunch for hot summer days when it's too hot to cook.

ingredients.name: oz. chopped kale (1/2 of a 12oz. bag) ($1.60)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: First, make the Lemon Dill Tahini Dressing. Add the garlic powder, onion powder, dried dill, dried parsley, salt, some freshly cracked pepper, tahini, warm water, and lemon juice to a bowl and whisk or stir until smooth.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Wash and finely chop the kale. Rinse and drain the can of cannellini beans. Drain the canned salmon. Thinly slice the red onion. If red onion tends to be too strong for you, soak them for a few minutes in ice water to mellow them out.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: To build the salad, fill each bowl with a couple large handfuls of the chopped kale. Top with 1/3 of the cannellini beans, 1/3 of the canned salmon, and a few slices of red onion. Drizzle the Lemon Dill Tahini Dressing over top and enjoy!

title: Chocolate Covered Peanut Butter Pretzel Bites
description: Stuffed with a creamy peanut butter filling and dipped in melted chocolate, these salty-sweet chocolate peanut butter pretzel bites are completely irresistible. I've never regretted making a double batch!
tags: chocolate covered peanut butter pretzel bites

ingredients.name: (28g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
(150g) packed light or dark brown sugar

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat. Set aside. Make sure you have enough room in your freezer for the baking sheet or use the refrigerator in step 3.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the peanut butter and butter together on medium speed. If you don’t have a mixer, just use a spoon and some arm muscle. Stir or beat in confectioners’ sugar and brown sugar until a thick dough forms.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Roll peanut butter dough into 30 small balls. Mixture can be sticky, so do your best. If it seems impossibly sticky, mix in 1/4 cup (30g) confectioners’ sugar.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Sandwich peanut butter balls between two pretzels and place on prepared baking sheet. Freeze for 20–30 minutes, or refrigerate for 1 hour.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: While the pretzel bites are chilling, melt chopped chocolate in a double boiler or in 20-second increments in the microwave, stopping and stirring after each increment until smooth. Dip the pretzel bites halfway into the melted chocolate and place bite back on baking sheet. Refrigerate pretzel bites for at least 10–20 minutes to set the chocolate.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Cover and store pretzel bites in the refrigerator for up to 10 days. Bites can be frozen for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator.

title: Apple Cider Glazed Pork Tenderloin
description: Apple cider is the secret to a marinade that makes pork tenderloin incredibly moist with a gorgeous glaze! The marinade also does double duty as the sauce. You will be amazed at the incredible flavour in this with so few ingredients!

ingredients.name: oz/330ml sweet apple cider ((alcoholic aka "hard cider") - I used Rochdale Apple Cider (Note 1))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Mix the Marinade ingredients in a bowl.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Place Marinade and pork in a large ziplock bag and marinate in the refrigerator overnight, up to 24 hours.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: To Cook

title: Creamy Mushroom and Spinach Orzo (Risoni) (One Pot)
description: An easy dinner for midweek all made in one pot. Super creamy but made without a single drop of cream! Nutrition grade rating of A-. Make it even more nutritious by adding more vegetables!

ingredients.name: / 250g frozen spinach ((1 box), thawed, excess water squeezed out)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Heat the olive oil in a large non stick pan over high heat (if you aren't using non stick, use more oil).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add the mushrooms and cook until softened (around 3 minutes). Remove from the pan and set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Lower the heat to medium and melt the butter in the pan. Add garlic and onion, and sauté until the onion becomes translucent - around 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add the flour, stir it in and cook for 1 minute.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add the milk and chicken broth and use a whisk to mix the butter flour mixture into the liquid.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Increase heat to medium high. Add the orzo/risoni, salt and pepper, stir once, then put a lid on (Note 2). Bring it to a simmer, then leave it to cook for around 8 to 10 minutes or until the orzo/risoni is cooked but still slightly firm, and it is still quite saucy. Note: If you have a strong stove, turn it down slightly so it just simmers).

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: While the orzo is cooking, break up the spinach into little pieces with your fingers.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Remove the pan from the stove. Stir through the parmesan, spinach and cooked mushrooms. Check if more salt or pepper is required.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Serve immediately, garnished with more parmesan and fresh finely chopped parsley, if desired.

title: Pork Tenderloin with Creamy Marsala Sauce
description: Juicy pork tenderloin smothered in a Creamy Marsala Sauce with Mushrooms. It's like gravy - but even better because the sauce has beautiful layers of flavour in it from the Marsala.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 200C/390F.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Sprinkle pork with salt and pepper.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Heat oil in an ovenproof skillet over high heat. Sear on all sides until nicely browned.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Place in the oven for 15 minutes for the faintest blush of pink (pictured) or 18 to 20 minutes for no pink. See Note 4 for internal cook temperatures.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Remove pork from skillet onto a plate, cover with foil and set aside in a warm place while you make the sauce.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Creamy Marsala Sauce

title: Lemony Artichoke and Quinoa Salad
description: This Lemony Artichoke and Quinoa Salad is a light and refreshing summer salad that holds up well in the refrigerator and is perfect for meal prep!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Rinse the quinoa well in a fine wire mesh sieve. Place the rinsed quinoa in a pot with 1.75 cups water. Place a lid on the pot, turn the heat on to high, and allow the water to come to a boil. Once it reaches a full boil, turn the heat down to low and let it simmer for 15 minutes (lid on).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: While the quinoa is cooking, prepare the lemon garlic dressing. Zest the lemon, then squeeze the juice (you'll need 1/4 to 1/3 cup juice). Add the lemon juice and 1 tsp of the zest to a bowl or jar along with the olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and some freshly cracked pepper (10-15 cranks of a pepper mill). Whisk the ingredients together or close the jar and shake until they are combined. Set the dressing aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: After the quinoa has cooked, spread it out into a casserole dish or other wide shallow dish and refrigerate uncovered for about 15 minutes to cool it down.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: While the quinoa is cooling, prepare the rest of the salad ingredients. Drain the artichoke hearts and roughly chop them into smaller pieces. Finely dice the red bell pepper. Roughly chop the parsley. Rinse and drain the chickpeas. Place the artichoke hearts, bell pepper, parsley, and chickpeas in a large bowl.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Once the quinoa has cooled, add it to the bowl with the other salad ingredients. Toss gently to combine, then drizzle the lemon garlic dressing over top. Gently toss again until everything is coated in dressing. Serve immediately or refrigerate up to 4-5 days.

title: Nutella-Stuffed Cinnamon Sugar Muffins
description: Donut-like muffins dipped in cinnamon-sugar and stuffed with Nutella.

ingredients.name: (71g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
and 1/2 cups (190g) white whole wheat flour* (spooned & leveled)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 425°F (218°C). Spray 12-count muffin pan with cooking spray and set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, cream butter and sugar together on medium speed. Mix in the egg, vanilla extract, and milk. Gently stir in flour, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt. Do not over-mix – stir until *just* combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Spoon 1 heaping Tablespoon of batter into muffin cup. Layer with 1 heaping teaspoon Nutella in the center and spoon another heaping Tablespoon of batter on top. If the muffin tins are completely full, that’s ok. Repeat layering batter and Nutella into each muffin cup. (Recipe makes about 8-9 muffins.) Fill the empty muffin cups halfway with water to help prevent pan from warping. (Optional, but it doesn’t hurt.)

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Bake at 425°F (218°C) for 5 minutes. Keeping the muffins in the oven, reduce temperature to 350°F (177°C) and bake for an additional 13-17 minutes until batter is set. Cool muffins in the pan for about 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: While the muffins are cooling, make the cinnamon-sugar topping. Melt the butter in the microwave. In a separate small bowl, stir the sugar and cinnamon together. Dip the top of each muffin into the melted butter, then dip into the cinnamon-sugar mixture. Swirl them around in the cinnamon-sugar a bit to make sure you get a thick coating. Set upright on cooling rack.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Muffins stay fresh in an airtight container up to 1 week.

title: Light Chocolate Chip Cheesecake Bars
description: An easy and delicious recipe for 128 calorie lightened-up chocolate chip cheesecake bars, sacrificing none of the creamy texture and flavor!

ingredients.name: 5-6 full-sheet graham crackers (or 3/4 cup graham cracker crumbs)
(28g) unsalted or salted butter, melted (or even melted coconut oil)
(226g) reduced fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature (do not use fat free)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 350°F (177°C). Line the bottom and sides of an 8-inch square baking pan with aluminum foil or parchment, leaving an overhang on all sides to easily lift the bars out of the pan before cutting. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Using a food processor or blender, pulverize the graham crackers into a fine crumb. Pour into a medium bowl and mix with melted butter. Press into the lined pan. Bake for 8 minutes. Allow to cool as you prepare the filling.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Beat the softened cream cheese for 1 minute using a hand or stand mixer. Beat in the yogurt, egg whites, sugar, and flour until mixture is smooth and creamy, about 3 full minutes. Add the lemon juice and vanilla, beat for 1 additional minute until combined. Fold in the chocolate chips.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Remove the crust from the oven and spread filling on top. Bake for 25 minutes, covering the cheesecake bars at the 20 minute mark with aluminum foil to prevent browning. Allow to cool for 20 minutes at room temperature, then chill completely in the refrigerator for at least three hours. Lift the foil out of the pan and cut into squares.

title: Meatloaf Cupcakes With Mashed Potato
description: Everything tastes better in miniature form! These Meatloaf Cupcakes are made extra irresistible by piling on mashed potato. These freeze very well. They are also great for hiding vegetables.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Meatloaf Cupcakes

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Mashed Potato

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Assemble

title: Sautéed Beef, Cabbage, and Rice
description: Sautéed Beef, Cabbage, and Rice is a simple yet delicious comfort food that will keep you full without draining your wallet.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Dice the onion and chop the cabbage into ½-inch pieces.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add the onion and cooking oil to a large skillet and sauté over medium heat until the onions are soft.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add the ground beef and steak seasoning to the skillet and continue to sauté until the beef is fully cooked. If using a higher fat content beef, drain the excess fat from the skillet.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add the chopped cabbage and water to the skillet. Continue to sauté until the cabbage has softened to your desired level of tenderness.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add the cooked rice and butter to the skillet. Fold the rice and butter into the beef and cabbage until the butter has melted, everything is evenly combined, and the rice is heated through.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Finally, season the skillet with soy sauce to taste. Start with 1 tsp and add more to your liking (I added ½ Tbsp, or 1.5 tsp). Serve hot and enjoy!

title: Tempeh Burrito Bowls
description: Spiced and crumbled tempeh makes a fast and easy alternative to beef in these Tempeh Burrito Bowls. Customize the toppings to be vegetarian or vegan!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 400ºF. Peel and dice the sweet potato into 1/2-inch cubes. Spread the cubes out onto a baking sheet, drizzle with oil, then toss to coat. Season with a pinch of salt.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Roast the sweet potatoes in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, stirring half way through. Once roasted, set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: While the sweet potatoes are roasting, prepare the seasoned tempeh. Crumble the brick of tempeh into a skillet. Add the water, oil, tomato paste, chili powder, smoked paprika, cumin, cayenne, oregano, garlic powder, salt, and some freshly cracked pepper (about 10 cranks of a pepper mill). Stir briefly to combine.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Place a lid on the skillet and turn the heat on to medium. Allow the skillet to come up to a simmer. Let the tempeh simmer, stirring every few minutes, for about 10 minutes total, or until the liquid reduces to a thick sauce.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: While the tempeh is simmering, slice the avocado and jalapeño.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: To build the bowls, place 1 cup cooked rice in the bottom of each bowl. Divide the seasoned tempeh, roasted sweet potatoes, corn, and avocado between all four bowls. Top each bowl with 1 to 2 tablespoons of salsa, 1 tablespoon of sour cream, and a few slices of jalapeño. Eat immediately or refrigerate for up to 4 days.

title: Soft & Fluffy Lemon Sweet Rolls
description: These soft and fluffy lemon sweet rolls are like a spring and summer version of classic cinnamon rolls. You'll love the caramelized lemon sugar inside and under the baked rolls!

ingredients.name: and 1/4 teaspoons Platinum Yeast from Red Star or any instant yeast (1 standard packet)
pure vanilla extract (or vanilla bean paste)
lemon zest (packed Tablespoon, it’s a lot of zest!)
(113g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature
(14g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
optional: extra lemon zest for garnish

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Whisk the flour, sugar, salt, and lemon zest together in a large bowl. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Combine the milk and butter together in a heatproof bowl. Microwave or use the stove and heat until the butter has melted and the mixture is warm to the touch (about 110°F/43°C, no higher). Whisk in the yeast until it has dissolved. Pour mixture into the dry ingredients, add the egg, and stir with a sturdy silicone spatula or wooden spoon, OR use a stand mixer with a paddle attachment on medium speed. Mix until a soft dough forms.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Transfer dough to a lightly floured surface. Using floured hands, knead the dough for 3–5 minutes. You should have a smooth ball of dough. If the dough is super soft or sticky, you can add a little more flour. Place in a lightly greased bowl (I use nonstick spray), cover loosely, and let the dough rest for about 10 minutes as you get the filling ingredients ready.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: After 10 minutes, roll the dough out in a 14×8-inch (36x20cm) rectangle. Spread the softened butter on top. Mix together the sugar, vanilla, and lemon zest. Sprinkle it all over the dough. Roll up the dough to make a 14-inch log. Cut into 10–12 even rolls and arrange in a lightly greased 9- or 10-inch round cake pan, pie dish, or square baking pan.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Cover the pan with aluminum foil, plastic wrap, or a clean kitchen towel. Allow the rolls to rise in a relatively warm environment for 60–90 minutes or until double in size. (For a tiny reduction in rise time, see my answer to Where Should Dough Rise? in my Baking With Yeast Guide.)

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: After the rolls have nearly doubled in size, preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake for 24–25 minutes, or until lightly browned. If you notice the tops are getting too brown too quickly, loosely tent the pan with aluminum foil and continue baking. If you want to be precise about their doneness, the rolls’ internal temperature taken with an instant-read thermometer should be around 195–200°F (91–93°C) when done. Remove pan from the oven and place pan on a wire rack as you make the icing. (You can also make the icing as the rolls bake.)

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: In a medium bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat the cream cheese on high speed until smooth and creamy. Add the butter and beat until smooth and combined, then beat in the confectioners’ sugar and lemon juice until combined. Using a knife or icing spatula, spread the icing over the warm rolls and serve immediately. If desired, garnish frosted rolls with a light sprinkling of lemon zest.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Cover leftover frosted or unfrosted rolls tightly and store at room temperature for up to 2 days, or in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: Honey Sriracha Chicken Skewers
description: Spice Level: MediumJust 5 ingredients is all you need to make a marinade that doubles as a fantastic glaze for these skewers! Just 20 minutes of marinating time required. A great balance of sweet, spicy and savoury.

ingredients.name: - 3 tbsp sriracha OR any other hot sauce ((e.g. Frank's), adjust amount to your taste)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Cut the chicken into 2cm / 4/5" cubes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Mix Marinade ingredients together in a bowl until smooth.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Transfer 4 tablespoons of the Marinade into a small bowl ("Reserved Marinade", to use for basting).

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add chicken into the bowl with the remaining Marinade. Mix to coat and set aside for 20 minutes to marinade (up to overnight). (Note 3)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Thread chicken onto skewers, discard marinade in the bowl. I used 14 small ones about 18cm/7" long, 4 pieces of chicken on each.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Heat oil on a BBQ or skillet over medium heat. Place skewers on the BBQ and cook the first side for 2 1/2 minutes, then turn the skewers.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Baste the COOKED side that is now facing up generously with the reserved Reserved Marinade. After 2 1/2 minutes, turn the skewers and quickly baste the other side generously with the Reserved Marinade. Turn immediately. Cook for just a few seconds, then transfer to a plate. (Note 4)

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Baste the skewers on the plate one more time with the Reserved Marinade, then serve.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: I served mine with Cilantro/Coriander Lime Rice (Note 5)

title: Italian Meatloaf with Marinara Sauce
description: My Italian meatloaf is made extra juicy by soaking bread in grated onion instead of using breadcrumbs.

ingredients.name: diced red bell pepper / capsicum ((1 large))
stale white bread (, crusts removed, roughly diced (about 3 slices))
/ 700 g tomato passata or crushed tinned tomatoes ((Note 1))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F. Spray a 28cm/11" loaf pan, then line with parchment / baking paper, leaving overhang (Note 2).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Heat oil over high heat. Add capsicum, leave for 1 minute without stirring so it starts to char (burn) then stir and continue cooking to char it more (about 2 minutes in total). Remove and set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Place bread in a large bowl. Grate onion into the bowl, using a normal box cutter (Note 3). Add cream, then use your hands to squidge the mixture together so the bread is completely soaked and starts to disintegrate.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add remaining meatloaf ingredients, including capsicum. Use your hands to mix it together until JUST combined. Do not over mix otherwise it will become dense.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Press into the loaf pan (Note 4). Drizzle with olive oil then bake for 30 minutes. Meanwhile, make the Sauce.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Take the meatloaf out of the oven. Carefully lift the meatloaf out of the loaf pan (using the paper overhang) onto a work surface. Then transfer the meatloaf into the skillet with the Sauce.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Pour juices in the loaf pan into the skillet. Return to the oven for a further 30 to 40 minutes, or until cooked to your liking.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Take it out of the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing. Serve the meatloaf with the Sauce, garnished with parmesan if desired.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: I like to serve this with mashed potatoes but it is also lovely with pasta.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Sauce

title: Piña Colada Cupcakes
description: Super-moist pineapple coconut cupcakes topped with creamy smooth coconut frosting. Decorate each cupcake with a sprinkle of coconut (toasted or not), a slice of pineapple, and a cherry on top. Have your piña colada and eat it too!

ingredients.name: (150g) packed light or dark brown sugar
optional: coconut, pineapple slices, and cherries for decoration
(12 Tbsp; 170g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 350°F (177°C). Line a 12-count muffin pan with cupcake liners. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a medium bowl, mix together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside. In a large microwave-safe bowl, melt butter in the microwave. Whisk in brown sugar and granulated sugar. Whisk in egg, pineapple juice, coconut milk, and vanilla extract until combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Slowly mix dry ingredients into the wet ingredients until no lumps remain. Fill cupcake liners 2/3 of the way full. Bake for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow to cool completely before frosting.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: In a medium bowl, beat the softened butter on medium speed with an electric or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment. Beat for about 3 minutes until smooth and creamy. Turn mixer off and add confectioners’ sugar, cream, vanilla, and coconut extracts. Mix on low for 1 minute then increase to high speed and beat for 3-4 full minutes. Add more confectioners’ sugar if frosting is too thin or more cream if frosting is too thick. Add salt if frosting is too sweet (I usually add 1/4 teaspoon). Frost cooled cupcakes. There may be leftover frosting depending how much you use on each cupcake. I used a Wilton #12 piping tip. Decorate cupcakes with coconut, pineapple slices, and/or a cherry.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Leftover cupcakes can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days and in the refrigerator up to 4.

title: Creamy Spinach Artichoke Chicken
description: Creamy Spinach Artichoke Chicken is a creamy and indulgent chicken dish that is fast and easy, but still impressive enough for date night.

ingredients.name: boneless, skinless chicken breasts (about 1-1.25 lb. total) ($6.29*)
12oz. jar quartered artichoke hearts in water ($2.79)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Place the chicken on a cutting board and cover with a sheet of plastic to prevent splatter. Use a mallet or rolling pin to gently pound the chicken out to an even thickness (about 3/4-inch thick). Slice each chicken breast in half to create two pieces each. Or, use thin sliced chicken breast filets. Season the breasts with a pinch of salt and pepper.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Heat the cooking oil in a large skillet over medium. Once hot, swirl the oil to coat the surface of the skillet. Add the chicken breasts and cook until golden brown on each side and cooked through (5-7 minutes each side).

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: While the chicken is cooking, roughly chop the spinach into smaller pieces. Drain the artichoke hearts and roughly chop them as well. Mince the garlic.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Once the chicken is cooked through, remove it to a clean plate and cover to keep warm. Add the butter and minced garlic to the skillet and continue to sauté over medium for one minute or just until the garlic begins to soften.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add the chicken broth to the skillet and stir to dissolve the browned bits from the bottom of the skillet. Cut the cream cheese into chunks and add it to the skillet along with the sour cream. Whisk the ingredients together and continue to cook over medium, whisking continuously, until the cream cheese is fully melted and the sauce is smooth. Add the milk and stir until smooth again.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Add the Parmesan, a pinch of red pepper flakes, and some freshly ground pepper to the skillet. Continue to heat and whisk for a couple minutes more. Finally, stir the spinach into the sauce, a handful or two at a time, until wilted (this should only take about a minute), then stir in the artichoke hearts.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Return the chicken to the skillet and spoon the creamy spinach artichoke sauce over top. Serve hot.

title: Orange Lemon Poppy Seed Muffins
description: My orange lemon poppy seed muffins are a twist on the original lemon version. Soft on the inside, slight crisp on the outside. And full of flavorful citrus!

ingredients.name: (120g) non-fat or low-fat Greek or regular yogurt, at room temperature

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 425°F (218°C). Spray 12-count muffin pan with nonstick spray. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a large bowl, whisk the flour, granulated sugar, poppy seeds, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together until thoroughly mixed. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: In a medium bowl, whisk the melted butter, lemon juice, lemon zest, orange juice, and orange zest together until combined. Add the eggs, one at a time, whisking after each addition. Whisk in the yogurt, milk, and vanilla. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and gently mix together until no pockets of flour remain. Do not over-mix. The batter is thick.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Spoon the thick batter into the muffin tins, filling them all the way to the top. Sprinkle each with coarse sugar. Bake for 5 minutes at 425°F (218°C). Keeping the muffins in the oven, reduce oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) and continue to bake for 10-13 minutes longer until tops are lightly golden. A toothpick inserted in the center should come out clean. Allow to cool for 10 minutes. Top with additional lemon and/or orange zest if desired (optional).

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Muffins stay fresh in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days. Try them with a simple lemon glaze too!

title: Cranberry Orange Muffins
description: Buttery, tender, and moist, these cranberry orange muffins are topped with a brown sugar crumb and sweet orange glaze. Both toppings are completely optional if you prefer plain cranberry orange muffins.

ingredients.name: (67g) packed light or dark brown sugar
(8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
(50g) packed light or dark brown sugar
(120g) plain yogurt or sour cream, at room temperature*
and 1/2 cups (185g) fresh or frozen cranberries (do not thaw)
optional: coarse sparkling sugar, for sprinkling on top (if not using crumb topping)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 425°F (218°C). Spray a 12-count muffin pan with nonstick spray or line with cupcake liners. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Mix the brown sugar, granulated sugar, cinnamon, and melted butter together in a small bowl until combined. Add the flour and use a fork to gently mix until crumbs form. Don’t over-mix into a paste. Just mix until it is crumbly. Place in the refrigerator while you make the muffin batter. Cold crumbs hold shape better when baking.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Whisk the flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt together in a medium bowl. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: In a large bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar together on high speed until smooth and creamy, about 3 minutes. Scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed. (Here’s a helpful tutorial if you need guidance on how to cream butter and sugar.) Add the orange zest and beat on medium speed for 1 minute. Add the eggs, yogurt/sour cream, and vanilla, and beat for 1 minute, then turn up to high speed until the mixture is combined and mostly creamy. (It’s ok if it appears somewhat curdled.) Scrape down the sides of the bowl as needed.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: With the mixer on low speed, add the dry ingredients, orange juice, and milk, and beat until no flour pockets remain. Gently fold in the cranberries. Avoid over-mixing. Batter is thick.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Spoon the batter into prepared muffin cups, filling them all the way to the top. Spoon crumb topping on each, gently pressing it down so it sticks. If not using crumb topping, sprinkle muffins evenly with coarse sugar.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Bake for 5 minutes at 425°F; then, without opening the oven, reduce the oven temperature to 350°F (177°C). Bake for an additional 16–19 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. The total time these muffins take in the oven is about 21–24 minutes. (Usually closer to 21 minutes if you’re skipping the crumb topping.)

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Allow the muffins to cool for 5 minutes in the muffin pan, then transfer to a wire rack to continue cooling. Let them cool for at least 15 minutes before icing.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Whisk the confectioners’ sugar and orange juice together and drizzle over warm or cooled muffins.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Iced or plain muffins stay fresh covered at room temperature for a few days or in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

title: Pull Apart Pizza Bread
description: Party food you can make ahead, freeze and can even be reheated in the microwave without it going soggy! Every piece of bread you pull out is like a mini pizza!

ingredients.name: garlic clove (, minced (or 2 if you like really garlicky))
baguette / french (, 60cm / 2 feet long (or multiple short ones))
to 1 1/2 cups grated mozzarella cheese ((Notes 1 and 2))
/ 120g pepperoni (, sliced or diced (I used mini pepperoni))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Melt butter with the garlic in it.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Cut the french stick into 1.5 - 2 cm / 3/5" - 4/5" thick slices (about 25 slices, including ends) and lay them out on a work surface.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Brush butter on each slice, ensuring to dab some garlic on. Use most but not all the butter.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Spread a small dab of pizza sauce on each piece of bread, top with onion, cheese then pepperoni (in this order).

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Stack 6 or 7 pieces of bread on top of each other. Then pick up the stack (hold the top and bottom pieces) and place it in a 22cm / 8" square baking dish. (See photos below) (Note 3)

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Brush the top of the bread with the remaining butter.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Cover with foil and bake for 15 minutes, or until cheese is mostly melted. Then remove the foil and bake for 10 minutes or until golden brown and crusty (to your liking).

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Remove from oven, sprinkle with parsley (if using) and serve immediately!

title: Parsley Pesto Pasta with Peas
description: Parsley Pesto Pasta with Peas has super bright and fresh flavors perfect for spring and summer. Serve as a main dish or a side with your grilled food.

ingredients.name: flat leaf Italian Parsley (about 2-3 cups, loosely packed) ($0.89)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Rinse the parsley well to remove any dirt or debris, then pat it dry with a clean, lint-free towel. Cut off the ends of the parsley stems and place the rest, leaves and stems, in the bowl of a food processor.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Zest and juice the lemon. Add half of the zest and about 2 Tbsp of the juice to the food processor, along with the Parmesan, garlic cloves (peeled), and some freshly cracked pepper (about 15 cranks of a pepper mill).

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Pulse the mixture until everything is finely minced and combined. Begin to add the olive oil in a thin stream through the chute of the processor as it blend the mixture together. Once the oil has been incorporated, add salt to taste. I added about 1/4 tsp salt.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Bring a pot of water to a boil for the pasta. Once boiling, add the pasta and continue to boil until the pasta is al dente. At the very end of the cook time, add the frozen peas and stir to combine. Let the peas sit in the hot water with the pasta for about a minute, then drain the pasta and peas in a colander.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Allow the pasta to cool just to the point where it is no longer steaming heavily. Transfer the drained pasta to a bowl and add the parsley pesto. Stir to combine, then serve warm. Add a pinch of the remaining lemon zest over top of each serving.

title: Cheesy Cottage Pie
description: This Cheesy Cottage Pie is chock-full of vegetables in a rich beef gravy, and topped with a layer of super smooth and creamy cheddar mashed potatoes.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Peel and chop the potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Rinse them in a colander with cool water, then place them in a pot and add enough fresh water to cover them completely. Add 1/2 tsp salt, place a lid on the pot, and bring it up to a boil over high heat. Boil the potatoes until very tender, about 7-10 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Drain the potatoes in a colander and rinse them again briefly with hot water. This removes excess starch and helps give the mashed potatoes a light and fluffy texture.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: While the potatoes are draining, add the butter and milk to the pot used to boil the potatoes. Heat the butter and milk over low until the butter is melted and the milk is hot. Add the drained potatoes back to the pot, then mash until smooth. Taste and add salt to taste (about 1/2 tsp). Stir in the shredded cheddar. Set the potatoes aside until you're ready to assemble the casserole.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: While the potatoes are cooking, begin to preheat the oven to 350ºF and begin the filling for the cottage pie. Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Add the onion and garlic to a large skillet along with the olive oil and sauté over medium until the onions are soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add the beef to the skillet and continue to sauté until the beef is fully cooked. Add the all-purpose flour and continue to stir and cook for about 2 minutes more, or until the flour coats the bottom of the skillet.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Add the beef broth and stir to dissolve the flour from the bottom of the skillet. The broth will come up to a simmer fairly quickly, at which point it will thicken into a gravy.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Add the Worcestershire sauce, rosemary, thyme, frozen peas and carrots, and frozen corn to the skillet. Heat through, then taste and add salt if needed (I added 1/2 tsp).

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Finally, assemble the cottage pie by transferring the beef and vegetable mixture to the bottom of a 2 quart casserole dish (mine was 9"x11"). Top with dollops of the warm cheesy mashed potatoes, spreading it out smooth to cover the surface.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Bake the Cheesy Cottage Pie for 30 minutes in the preheated 350ºF oven. If you want extra browning on top, switch the oven to broil after baking and allow it to broil for a few minutes or until the desired browning has been achieved. Let the casserole rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.

title: Feta Marinated with Herbs and Chilli
description: A cost effective starter that tastes as delightful as it looks and takes minutes to prepare.

ingredients.name: / 250 g feta cheese (, preferably Danish or another creamy type (see notes))
herbs (, finely chopped. (I used parsley and thyme leaves))
birds eye chilli (, finely chopped (or any fresh red chilli of choice))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Infuse olive oil with garlic (optional): use the side of your knife to smash the garlic cloves and combine with the olive oil. Cover, refrigerate and leave for as long as possible - a few hours or preferably overnight.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: If you do not have the time to do Step 1, just mince 1 garlic clove (small) and stir into the olive oil.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Cut the feta into bite size cubes - about 1cm / 1/3 inch.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Place feta into serving dish, scatter over herbs, chilli, lemon rind and a grind of black pepper.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Pour over olive oil. Serve with crusty bread.

title: Classic Mint Chocolate Brownies
description: Thick and fudgy brownies layered with sweet mint frosting and easy chocolate ganache. The classic mint chocolate combination!

ingredients.name: (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
optional: 1 drop liquid or gel green food coloring

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Melt the butter and chopped chocolate in a medium saucepan on medium heat, stirring constantly, about 5 minutes. Or melt in a medium microwave safe bowl in 20 second increments, stirring after each, in the microwave. Remove from the heat, pour into a large mixing bowl, and allow to slightly cool for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Adjust the oven rack to the lower third position and preheat oven to 350°F (177°C). Line the bottom and sides of a 9×13-inch baking pan* with parchment paper, leaving an overhang on all sides. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Whisk the granulated and brown sugars into the cooled chocolate/butter mixture. Add the eggs, one at a time, whisking until smooth after each addition. Whisk in the vanilla. Gently fold in the salt, flour, and cocoa powder. Pour batter into the prepared baking pan and bake for 35-36 minutes or until the brownies begin to pull away from the edges of the pan. A toothpick inserted in the center should come out with only a few moist crumbs when the brownies are done. All ovens are different, so keep an eye on them after 30 minutes and use the toothpick test to see when yours are finished. Allow the brownies to cool completely in the pan set on a wire rack. Once completely cooled, lift the foil out of the pan using the overhang on the sides. Place the whole thing on a baking sheet as you make the frosting. Do not cut into squares yet.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: In a medium bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on medium speed until smooth and creamy, about 2 minutes. Add the confectioners’ sugar and milk. Beat for 2 minutes on low speed, then increase to high speed and beat for 1 additional minute. Add the peppermint extract and food coloring (if using) and beat on high for 1 full minute. Taste and add a drop or two more peppermint extract if desired. Frost cooled brownies, that you placed on the baking sheet, and place the baking sheet in the refrigerator. This allows the frosting to “set” on top of the brownies which makes spreading the chocolate layer easy. Keep in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour and up to 4 hours.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Melt the butter and chocolate chips in a medium saucepan on medium heat, stirring constantly, about 5 minutes. Or melt in a medium microwave safe bowl in 20 second increments, stirring after each, in the microwave. Once melted and smooth, pour over mint layer. Gently spread with a knife or offset spatula. Place the brownies, that are still on the baking sheet, in the refrigerator and chill for 1 hour (and up to 4 hours or even overnight) to set the chocolate.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Once chilled, remove from the refrigerator and cut into squares. For neat cutting, make very quick cuts, using a very sharp large knife, and wiping the knife off with a paper towel between each cut. Brownies are OK at room temperature for a few hours. Cover tightly and keep leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: Prosciutto Wrapped Chicken and Scalloped Potatoes (One Pan!)
description: This is one of those recipes that is easy enough for midweek but elegant enough for company - at least, in my opinion!

ingredients.name: Zest of 1 small lemon (, or 1/2 large lemon)
chicken thigh fillets (, skinless and boneless (about 5 - 7 oz / 150 - 200g each))
long or 8 small thin slices of prosciutto
/ 750 g potatoes (, peeled and cut into thin slices)
olive oil + 1/2 tbsp extra olive oil

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Mix together the Ricotta Filling ingredients in a small bowl.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Lay out the prosciutto on a work surface. Place chicken lengthwise at the bottom of each strip.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Divide Ricotta Filling between the chicken, placing it at the bottom (closest to you).

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Roll up the chicken, wrapping it in the prosciutto, finishing with the seam side down. Not too tightly otherwise the ricotta will ooze out!

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Use toothpicks to seal the ends. (Note 2)

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Spread the potato slices on the base of a roasting dish or large ovenproof skillet. Fan them out so they are overlapping slightly.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Mix together 1 1/2 tbsp olive oil and garlic then pour over the potatoes, using a teaspoon or brush to spread it out. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and thyme leaves.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Place chicken on top of the potato, drizzle the chicken with 1/2 tbsp olive oil and bake for 40 minutes.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Remove chicken onto a plate to rest, covered loosely with foil.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Sprinkle the potatoes with parmesan cheese, then return just the potatoes back into the oven and turn up the oven to maximum. Bake for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 12
steps.description: Remove potatoes from the oven. Place chicken back on top.

steps.title: Step 13
steps.description: Serve asap!

title: Shredded Sweet Soy Chicken Breast
description: A simple, fast, very tasty way to prepare chicken. Chicken breast is perfect for shredding. Because it's so lean, it is especially great combined with a jammy intense flavoured sauce that is sweet and sour. Add a kick of chili if you want!

ingredients.name: oil ((vegetable, canola, olive oil or grapeseed oil))
white vinegar ((or substitute with other vinegar except balsamic))
black peppercorns or 1/2 tbsp ground black pepper ((Note 2))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Slow Cooker: Place all the Shredded Chicken ingredients in the slow cooker and cook on low for 5 hours (or 2 1/2 hours on high).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Pressure Cooker: Place all the Shredded Chicken ingredients in the pressure cooker. Cook for 35 minutes on high.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Stove: Place all the Shredded Chicken ingredients in a saucepan large enough for the chicken to fit snugly in one layer. Add 1/2 cup extra water so the chicken is mostly, but not completely submerged. Cover and simmer for about 50 to 60 minutes, turning once or twice.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: The chicken is ready when it can be easily shredded with two forks. Remove the chicken from the liquid and shred it while it is warm. Set aside and cover to keep warm.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Transfer the liquid from the slow cooker / pressure cooker to a saucepan and bring to simmer. (Note 3) Reduce to around1 1/4 cups - it should only take a few minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Mix together the Sauce Thickening ingredients and add to the saucepan. Simmer until it it thickens to a syrup consistency.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Toss the chicken in the sauce and serve over rice. (Note 4)

title: 45 Calorie Mini Chocolate Chip Muffins
description: These 45 calorie mini chocolate chip muffins left me incredibly surprised. A low calorie mini muffin that is also tender, soft, and full of flavor. Plus... lots of chocolate.

ingredients.name: (50g) lightly packed light or dark brown sugar
(28g) butter or coconut oil, melted and slightly cooled*

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 350°F (177°C). Spray two mini muffin pans with nonstick cooking spray. This recipe makes around 36 muffins, so you will only use half of the 2nd pan. I do not recommend mini liners for these. Set pan aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a large bowl, gently toss the flour, baking soda, and cinnamon together. Set aside. In a separate bowl, whisk the maple syrup, brown sugar, yogurt, butter, beaten egg white, and vanilla together until no brown sugar lumps remain and everything is combined. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir with a silicone spatula or large wooden spoon until *just* combined—do not overmix. Fold in the mini chocolate chips.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Fill muffin cups only about halfway—these muffins rise! A couple teaspoons of batter per cup is plenty. Bake for 12-14 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. If your oven has hot spots, be sure to rotate the pans during bake time. My muffins took 12 minutes. Remove pans from the oven. Allow muffins to slightly cool before enjoying.

title: Scallion Herb Chickpea Salad
description: Light, bright, and full of spring flavors, this Scallion Herb Chickpea Salad will keep you full all afternoon. Serve as a sandwich, wrap, or salad!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Place the avocado, yogurt, lemon juice, salt, parsley, cilantro, and scallions in a bowl. Mash the ingredients together until combined. It's okay if the avocado is a bit chunky still.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Drain the can of chickpeas, then add them to the bowl with the avocado dressing. Stir and mash the chickpeas into the dressing. Taste the salad and add salt if needed.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Serve the salad over a bed of greens, between two slices of bread, in a pita, or wrapped in a tortilla.

title: Bacon Wrapped Hot Dogs with Cheese Sauce
description: Full loaded hot dogs with bacon wrapped hot dogs, onions and smothered with a fantastic cheese sauce that stays creamy even at room temperature.

ingredients.name: grated cheddar or tasty cheese (, or any other good melting cheese)
strips of streaky bacon (, long enough to wrap each hot dog)
to othpicks (, soaked in water for 30 minutes)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Cheese Sauce

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Hot Dogs

title: Strawberry Buttermilk Pancakes with Honey Butter
description: Fluffy buttermilk pancakes loaded with vanilla flavor and juicy strawberries. Top with homemade honey butter for the ultimate weekend indulgence!

ingredients.name: (85g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled (plus more butter for greasing)
honey butter and maple syrup for serving

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Melt the 6 Tablespoons (85g) of butter first. Microwave or stovetop—either is fine. Set aside to slightly cool. You don’t want it piping hot.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a large bowl, preferably with a pour spout, toss the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together until combined. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: In another large bowl, whisk the eggs, buttermilk, and vanilla extract together until combined. Whisk in the melted butter. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and gently whisk to combine. Make sure there are no patches of dry flour at the bottom of the bowl. The batter is thick and a few lumps are fine. Very gently, fold in the strawberries.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium heat. Coat generously with butter or nonstick cooking spray. Once it’s hot, drop/pour a heaping 1/4 cup of batter on the griddle. Cook until the edges look set and you notice holes in the pancake’s surface around the border, about 2 minutes. Flip and cook the other side until cooked through, about 1–2 more minutes. Coat griddle/skillet with butter or nonstick spray again, if needed, for each batch of pancakes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Keep pancakes warm in a preheated 200°F (93°C) oven until all pancakes are cooked. Serve pancakes immediately with honey butter and maple syrup. (Though I love them even without the maple syrup!)

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Cover and store leftover pancakes in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: Salsa Chicken Meal Prep Bowls
description: These easy Salsa Chicken Meal Prep Bowls are easy to customize to fit your diet or macros, and are 1/3 the cost of a meal delivery service.

ingredients.name: boneless, skinless chicken breasts (about 1.3 lbs. total) ($5.62)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 425ºF. Cook the brown rice according to the package directions. (Add rice, salt, and water to a pot, cover, bring to a boil over high heat, then turn the heat down to low and simmer for 35 minutes.)

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: While the rice is cooking, begin the chicken. Add the chicken breasts to a medium sauce pot along with the salsa, chicken broth, and chili powder. Give everything a brief stir.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Place a lid on the pot and bring it to a boil over high heat. Once the liquid starts boiling, turn the heat down to low and let the chicken simmer over low for 30 minutes. Make sure it's simmering the whole time, adjusting the heat slightly if needed.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: While the rice and chicken are cooking, prepare the bell peppers. Slice the bell peppers into 1/2-wide strips. Place them on a baking sheet and drizzle with cooking oil. Toss the peppers to coat them in oil, then sprinkle with a pinch of salt.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Roast the peppers in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until they are browned on the edges, stirring once half way through.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: After the chicken has simmered for 30 minutes, remove it from the salsa mixture and use two forks to shred the meat. Return the shredded chicken to the pot of salsa and stir to combine.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Once the rice has rested, the chicken has been shredded, and the peppers have finished roasting, it's time to build the bowls.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Add about 3/4 cup rice to each container, followed by 1/4 of the roasted peppers, and 1/4 of the shredded chicken. Spoon the salsa mixture from the pot over the shredded chicken in the containers. This will act as a sauce to help moisten the entire dish. Top with sliced green onions and a dollop of sour cream and serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 4 days.

title: Spicy Italian Breakfast Roll Ups (Breakfast On The Go / Mini Taquitos)
description: Great food on the go, freezable and microwavable. These are made using soft tacos (small tortillas) so they are a great size for eating on the move with your hands.

ingredients.name: small onion (, diced (brown, yellow, white, spanish))
birds eye chillis (, sliced (adjust amount to taste - Note 2))
grated cheese ((I used cheddar, any other flavoured cheese that melts works fine))
fresh basil leaves (, thinly sliced (or sub with 1 tbsp dried Italian herbs - basil, parsley, oregano))
small tortillas (, corn or flour (around 6" / 12 cm rounds))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F. Spray a baking tray with oil, or line with parchment/baking paper.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Remove sausages from casings.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Heat oil in a large fry pan over high heat. Add the sausage and cook, breaking up into chunky pieces, until nicely browned. Remove from pan.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Turn stove down to medium high and melt butter in the same pan (don't clean the pan). Add the onion, chills and garlic and sauté for 3 to 5 minutes until the onion is translucent and a light golden brown.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add the eggs, milk, salt and pepper. Stir slowly, not vigorously, using a wooden spoon until the eggs are "holding together" but are still wet - about 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Remove pan from the stove, stir through the sausage, cheese and basil leaves.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Divide filling between the tortillas.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Roll up the tortillas and place on the baking tray, seam side down. Brush with olive oil (optional), then bake for 15 to 20 minutes until light golden and slightly crisp.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Serve while warm.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: To freeze, allow to cool then place in the freezer. To reheat, defrost then reheat in the oven (to make it crisp again) or in the microwave (the tortilla will reheat soft so it will be more like a burrito).

title: Slow Cooker Cinnamon Rolls
description: Baking cinnamon rolls in a slow cooker cuts down on preparation time. The dough does not have to rise twice. In fact, it doesn't even need to rise at all! All of the rising and baking is done in the slow cooker.
