ingredients.name: packet Red Star Platinum Yeast or any instant yeast (1 packet = 2 and 1/4 teaspoons)
(4 Tbsp; 56g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
(30ml) pure maple syrup (or use 1 teaspoon vanilla extract)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Warm the milk over on the stove over low heat or microwave it until lukewarm. No need to use a thermometer, but to be precise: about 95°F (35°C). Pour the warm milk into the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the dough hook or paddle attachment (OR you can use a handheld mixer). Whisk in the yeast and 1 teaspoon granulated sugar. Cover with a clean towel and let sit until the yeast is foamy, about 5-10 minutes. This is called proofing the yeast. If the yeast does not dissolve and foam, start over with fresh active yeast. On low speed, beat in the remaining 1/4 cup sugar, salt, butter, egg, and 2 cups of flour until combined. The dough will be wet. While continuing to beat on low speed, add the remaining flour 1/4 cup at a time until a soft dough forms. Dough will be ready when it gently pulls away from the sides of the bowl. I usually use 2 and 3/4 cups flour total. **If you do not have a mixer, you can stir the dough by hand with a wooden spoon in this step.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Keep the dough in the mixer and beat for an additional 5 full minutes, or knead by hand on a lightly floured surface for 5 full minutes. (If you’re new to bread-baking, my How to Knead Dough video tutorial can help here.) If the dough becomes too sticky during the kneading process, sprinkle 1 teaspoon of flour at a time on the dough or on the work surface/in the bowl to make a soft, slightly tacky dough. Do not add more flour than you need because you do not want a dry dough. After kneading, the dough should still feel a little soft. Poke it with your finger—if it slowly bounces back, your dough is ready to rise. You can also do a “windowpane test” to see if your dough has been kneaded long enough: tear off a small (roughly golfball-size) piece of dough and gently stretch it out until it’s thin enough for light to pass through it. Hold it up to a window or light. Does light pass through the stretched dough without the dough tearing first? If so, your dough has been kneaded long enough and is ready to rise. If not, keep kneading until it passes the windowpane test.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Let the dough rest for about 10 minutes. During this 10 minutes, line a 4 or 5 quart slow cooker with greased parchment paper. (I spray it with non-stick spray, but brushing oil or softened butter all over it works too.) If you have a larger slow cooker, that’s ok—just space the rolls out more.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: After 10 minutes, roll the dough out in a 14×8-inch rectangle. Spread the softened butter on top. Mix the cinnamon and sugar together, then sprinkle on top of the butter. Roll the dough up tightly, then cut into 10-12 even pieces and place them inside the lined slow cooker. Place a paper towel right under the lid of the slow cooker—this will help keep condensation off of the cooking rolls. Do not leave that step out and do not use a cloth towel.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Turn your slow cooker on high speed and cook for 2 hours, or until the rolls are fully cooked through. It’s usually 2 hours, sometimes 2 hours and 15 minutes. Once they’re done, remove the rolls from the cooker right away by lifting out the parchment paper.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Right before serving, top your cinnamon rolls with glaze. Whisk the confectioners’ sugar, maple syrup, and milk together until smooth. Add a little more milk if too thick. Drizzle over the warm rolls.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Rolls are best enjoyed the same day, but stay fresh covered tightly in the refrigerator for 5 days. Rolls (with or without icing) freeze well up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and heat up however you prefer.

title: Cheesy Jalapeno Bacon Stuffed Baguette with Garlic Butter
description: A new idea for party food! This is great for making ahead. Just assemble then pop it in the freezer until you need it, then all you have to do is bake them when you are ready to serve!

ingredients.name: / 250g Philadelphia cream cheese, softened (full or low fat)
packed jalapeños, finely chopped (if using fresh, deseed it. Or use canned, drained)
finely chopped scallions/shallots, green and white part (packed)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Heat oil in a pan over high heat. Add bacon and cook until lightly browned. Remove onto a plate lined with a paper towel to drain excess fat. Set aside to cool.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Cut the ends off the baguette and cut the baguette in half (or quarters - makes it easier to stuff).

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Use a long knife (preferably bread knife) to cut the bread out, leaving about a 1/2" / 1.5cm wall of bread and crust. (Note 2)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Place the bacon, cream cheese, cheddar and mozzarella cheese, jalapeños, scallions/shallots and pepper in a bowl and mix until combined.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Use a knife or piping bag to stuff the mixture inside the baguettes.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Garlic Butter: melt butter with the garlic and salt in the microwave or on the stove.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Brush the baguette with garlic butter (or you can do this after you slice it).

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Slice the baguette into 1 1/4cm / 1/2" slices.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Keep the slices pressed up against each other, and transfer to a large piece of foil.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Wrap the baguette, then bake for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the bread is crusty and the cheese inside is melted.

steps.title: Step 12
steps.description: For optimal experience, serve warm!

title: Homemade Ham & Cheese Pockets
description: Make freezer-friendly homemade ham & cheese pockets with this easy recipe!

ingredients.name: and 1/4 teaspoons (7g) Platinum Yeast from Red Star instant yeast (1 standard packet)*
(30ml) olive oil, plus more for pan and brushing on dough
egg wash: 1 large egg beaten with 1 Tablespoon milk
cheese or 2 cups shredded (I use sharp cheddar)
thin slices deli ham or 2 cups cubed ham

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Whisk the warm water, yeast, and granulated sugar together in the bowl of your stand mixer fitted with a dough hook or paddle attachment. Cover and allow to rest for 5 minutes. *If you don’t have a stand mixer, simply use a large mixing bowl and mix the dough with a wooden spoon or silicone spatula in the next step.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add the olive oil, salt, and flour. Beat on low speed for 2 minutes until combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Keep the dough in the mixer and beat for an additional 5 full minutes, or knead by hand on a lightly floured surface for 5 full minutes. (If you’re new to bread-baking, my How to Knead Dough video tutorial can help here.) If the dough becomes too sticky during the kneading process, sprinkle 1 teaspoon of flour at a time on the dough or on the work surface/in the bowl to make a soft, slightly tacky dough. Do not add more flour than you need because you do not want a dry dough. After kneading, the dough should still feel a little soft. Poke it with your finger—if it slowly bounces back, your dough is ready to rise. You can also do a “windowpane test” to see if your dough has been kneaded long enough: tear off a small (roughly golfball-size) piece of dough and gently stretch it out until it’s thin enough for light to pass through it. Hold it up to a window or light. Does light pass through the stretched dough without the dough tearing first? If so, your dough has been kneaded long enough and is ready to rise. If not, keep kneading until it passes the windowpane test.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Lightly grease a large bowl with oil or nonstick spray—just use the same bowl you used for the dough. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it to coat all sides in the oil. Cover the bowl with aluminum foil, plastic wrap, or a clean kitchen towel. Allow the dough to rise at room temperature for 60-90 minutes or until double in size. (Tip: For the warm environment on a particularly cold day, heat your oven to 150°F (66°C). Turn the oven off, place the dough inside, and keep the door slightly ajar. This will be a warm environment for your dough to rise. After about 30 minutes, close the oven door to trap the air inside with the rising dough. When it’s doubled in size, remove from the oven.)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Preheat oven to 400°F (204°C). Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Once risen, punch the dough down to release the air. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Using a sharp knife, slice dough in half. Then slice each half into quarters. You’ll have 8 pieces of dough, as shown in the photo above. Flatten each piece with your fingers or use a rolling pin. You want each piece of flattened dough to be about 6 inches long and 4 inches wide. They don’t have to be perfect rectangles, as you can see in my photos! Transfer flattened dough to the baking sheets. Place the cheese (I fold the slice in half) and ham (2 slices for each) on top of the dough on one side, then fold the other side over top. Pinch the edges to seal as best you can. Use a fork to crimp the edges. Brush the tops and sides of each pocket with egg wash, then slice 2-3 slits in the tops for air vents (use a super sharp knife to do that).

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Bake the pockets for 20-25 minutes, or until the tops are golden brown. Make sure to rotate the baking sheet once or twice during bake time. Remove pockets from the oven and allow to cool on the pan until ready to handle and serve.

title: Sun Dried Tomato, Kale, and White Bean Skillet
description: This quick Sun Dried Tomato, Kale, and White Bean Skillet is a fast, flavorful, and fiber-licious meal that is ready for perfect for weekly meal prepping.

ingredients.name: 7oz. jar sun dried tomatoes in oil ($2.14)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Drain and rinse the white beans. Roughly chop the sun dried tomatoes. In a small bowl, combine 2 Tbsp of the oil from the jar of sun dried tomatoes with the apple cider vinegar, basil, salt, pepper, and a pinch of red pepper flakes. Set the dressing aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add the olive oil and minced garlic to a large skillet. Sauté the garlic over medium heat for one minute, or just until it begins to soften and becomes fragrant.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add the chopped kale and 2 Tbsp water to the skillet. Stir and cook over medium heat until the kale has wilted.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add the drained beans and chopped sun dried tomatoes to the skillet with the kale. Continue to stir and cook until the beans are heated through. Lastly, drizzle the dressing over the skillet and stir to combine, and add salt to taste. Serve warm.

title: Vegetable Breakfast Scrambles
description: These Vegetable Breakfast Scrambles are a savory breakfast lover's dream. Fast, flavorful, full of healthy vegetables, and fully customizable.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Add the butter to a large non-stick skillet and heat over medium. Once melted, add the diced bell pepper and sauté for 1-2 minutes. Add the spinach and sauté just until wilted (about one minute).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Push the vegetable to the sides and crack the eggs in the center. Add the seasoning of your choice. Keeping the skillet over medium heat, begin to scramble the eggs, mixing them with the vegetables. When the eggs are mostly set, remove the skillet from the heat.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add the cooked eggs and vegetables to a bowl and top with sliced tomatoes, avocado, hummus, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

title: Margarita Cupcakes
description: Margarita cupcakes begin with a simple sweet lime cupcake base and are topped with fluffy tequila lime frosting!

ingredients.name: (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
zest + fresh juice of 3 medium limes*
optional: 2 Tablespoons tequila (for brushing in step 5)
(16 Tbsp; 226g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
lime flavoring (please see notes—there are options)*
optional: lime slices, lime zest, and/or coarse sugar for garnish

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 350°F (177°C). Line a 12-count muffin pan with cupcake liners.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Whisk the flour, baking powder, and salt together in a large bowl. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: With a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat the butter on high speed in a large bowl until smooth and creamy, about 1 minute. Add the granulated sugar and beat on high speed for 2 minutes until creamed. Scrape down the sides and up the bottom of the bowl as needed. With the mixer running on low speed, add the eggs one at a time, then add the vanilla extract. Beat on medium-high speed until combined. Scrape down the sides and up the bottom of the bowl as needed.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: With the mixer running on low speed, slowly add some of the dry ingredients alternating with some of the milk until all is mixed. Increase to high speed and beat in the lime zest and juice until incorporated. Try to avoid overmixing.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Pour/spoon the batter into the liners—fill only 1/2 – 2/3 full to avoid spilling over the sides. Bake for 18-20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. For around 30 mini cupcakes, bake for about 11-13 minutes, same oven temperature. While still warm, brush finished cupcakes with tequila. This infuses extra tequila flavor. Allow the cupcakes to cool completely before frosting.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: In a large bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, beat the butter on high speed until completely smooth and creamy, about 2-3 minutes. Add the confectioners’ sugar 1 cup at a time, beating on low at first then increasing to high speed. Once incorporated, add the next cup. Once creamy and combined, beat in the tequila, vanilla extract, lime flavoring, and pinch of salt on high speed. Taste. Add more salt or flavoring as needed.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Frost cooled cupcakes. I used a Wilton 1M tip for these photos. (Another favorite is this Wilton #12 round tip.) Garnish with lime slices, lime zest, or coarse sugar.

title: BBQ Beef and Cabbage
description: This quick BBQ Beef and Cabbage comes together in one skillet and uses inexpensive cabbage to bulk out the meal instead of relying on pasta or rice.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Add the olive oil and ground beef to a large skillet and cook over medium heat. Begin to break up the beef as it browns.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: While the beef is browning, finely dice the onion. Add the onion, paprika, garlic powder, and salt to the skillet with the ground beef and continue to cook over medium heat until the onions are soft.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: While the onions and beef are cooking, finely shred the cabbage. Once the onions are soft, add 1/4 cup water to the skillet and stir to dissolve any browned bits off the bottom of the skillet. Add the shredded cabbage and stir to combine. Place a lid on the skillet and let simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cabbage has wilted.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add the tomato sauce, vinegar, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, and Dijon to the skillet. Stir to combine and let simmer a few more minutes without the lid, or until the liquid in the skillet has reduced slightly. Taste the sauce and add more salt if needed (I added 1/4 tsp).

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Finally, top the beef and cabbage with the shredded cheddar, place the lid back on the skillet for 1-2 minutes, or until the cheese has melted. Top with sliced green onions then serve.

title: Homemade Funnel Cakes
description: Here's exactly how to make homemade funnel cakes! You only need a few basic ingredients to get started.

ingredients.name: optional for flavor: 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Pour oil into the pot set over medium heat. Heat to 375°F (191°C). I usually prepare the batter (next step) as the oil is heating.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Whisk the flour, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon together in a large bowl. Set aside. In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs, brown sugar, vanilla, and milk. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and whisk until no lumps remain.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Place a wire rack over a baking sheet. This is where the cakes can cool immediately after frying.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: If using a funnel, cover the bottom of the spout with your finger as you pour the batter in. Measure about 1/3 cup of batter and pour into the hot oil, making one large swirl and making sure to connect some of the swirl layers. It doesn’t have to be pretty, it doesn’t have to be perfect! Fry until golden brown, about 1-2 minutes on each side. Carefully remove with a metal spatula or metal slotted spoon and place onto the wire rack to slightly cool. Repeat with remaining batter, then turn off heat.*

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Serve the funnel cakes while they’re still a little warm. Top with favorites like confectioners’ sugar, whipped cream, chocolate syrup, caramel, or sprinkles. Cakes are best enjoyed the same day, though they keep in the refrigerator for a couple extra days.

title: Hasselback Baked French Toast
description: A french toast bake unlike any other! Using baguettes (french stick bread) soaked in an egg mixture, this comes out golden and crusty on the outside and custardy on the inside.

ingredients.name: baguette / french stick (, 60 cm / 2 feet long (Note 1))
milk ((preferably whole fat but low fat works just fine))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Cut the french stick loaf into 3 equal pieces.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Cut slices into each piece around 1.5cm / 0.5" thick almost through to the base (like hasselback potatoes / classic garlic bread).

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Place the Egg Mixture into a bowl and whisk to combine.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Place the Egg Mixture and bread into a ziplock bag or plastic bag. Remove as much air as you can and seal. Set aside for at least 1 hour (or overnight), turning once or twice, or until most of the Egg Mixture has been soaked up by the bread. (Note 1)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Place the bread into a baking dish (22 x 22 cm / 8 x 8"), cut side up.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Place in the oven and bake for 20 minutes.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Place the Butter Mixture into a small saucepan over medium heat. Heat until melted and combined, stirring occasionally. You could do this in the microwave too - around 2 x 30 second bursts, stirring in between.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Remove the french toast from the oven. Pour the butter mixture over the french toast and return to oven. Bake for a further 5 to 10 minutes or until golden and crispy on top.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Remove from oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Sift over icing sugar (confectioners sugar) and serve with maple syrup.

title: Kale Salad with Toasted Pita and Parmesan
description: Fresh salads are not just for summer! This Kale Salad with Toasted Pita and Parmesan features winter flavors, but is still light, fresh, and crunchy!

ingredients.name: shredded kale, loosely packed (about one bunch) ($0.99)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Pull the kale leaves from the stems. Take a handful at a time in one fist, then carefully pull back your fingers one inch at a time as you slice the bunched kale into fine shreds. Place the shredded kale in a colander and rinse well. Allow the kale to dry as you prepare the remaining ingredients.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Cut the pita into 1-inch triangles. Place the pita triangles in a small bowl and drizzle with olive oil. Toss the pita until it is well coated in oil. Heat a small skillet over medium, then once the skillet is hot, add the pita. Cook and stir the pita until they are toasted and crunchy.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Remove the pita from the skillet, then add the chopped walnuts. Stir and cook the walnuts in the dry skillet for 1-2 minutes, or just until they begin to smell toasty. Remove them from the skillet immediately to prevent burning.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: To assemble the salad, place the washed and dried kale in a large bowl. Add the toasted pita, walnuts, Parmesan, and golden raisins. Pour the champagne vinaigrette over top, then toss until everything is coated in dressing and Parmesan.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Serve the salad immediately, or let it rest for 10-15 minutes to allow the dressing to slightly soften the kale.

title: Double Cheese and Bacon Rolls
description: Not only topped but also STUFFED with bacon and cheese! Just like the ones from Baker's Delight (bakery chain in Australia) - but better because there's a double hit of bacon and cheese!

ingredients.name: sachet Active Dry Yeast ((7g, 1/4oz, 2 1/4 tsp (US, AUS), 2 tsp UK) (see Note 1 for Instant Yeast))
caster sugar ((or granulated or super fine white sugar))
warm water ((Not boiling. Just tap water is fine))
olive oil ((or sub with any other oil))
/ 100g provolone cheese (, cut into 8 slices (or any other melting cheese) (Note 3))
grated cheese ((tasty, cheddar or any other cheese that melts))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Combine the yeast, sugar and water in a bowl and mix until yeast dissolves. Set aside in a warm place for 10 minutes until frothy. (Note 4)

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Combine the flour and salt in a bowl. Add the yeast mixture and olive oil. Then either use the dough hook on your mixer and mix for about 3 minutes on medium low, or knead by hand on a lightly floured surface (about 100 kneads), until the dough is smooth and elastic. It should come away from the side of the bowl and shouldn't stick. If it is too sticky, add some extra flour.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Place the dough in a bowl and cover loosely. (Note 5) Set aside in a warm place for 1 hour or until doubled in size. (Note 4)

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Meanwhile, sauté half the bacon in a non stick pan over high heat until lightly browned. Remove from pan and set aside to cool.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Spray baking tray with oil.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Punch down the dough, then cut into 8 pieces.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Get one piece of dough and flatten it. Take a piece of provolone cheese and fold it in half to form a square. Top with 1 tbsp of cooked bacon. Fold the edges in to "wrap" it, shaping it into a round shape. Seal then flip over and place on baking try. Flatten with your hand to around 2cm / 4/5" thick. Repeat with remaining dough.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Brush with egg mixture. Top each with 1 1/2 tbsp grated cheese and the uncooked bacon.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Cover with clean tea towel and set aside for 30 minutes or so in a warm place until it has risen by around 50% (Note 6).

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until they are golden brown.

steps.title: Step 12
steps.description: Best served warm, so the filling is gooey!

title: Blackberry Lemon Poppy Seed Muffins
description: Light and fluffy blackberry lemon poppy seed muffins are on a whole new level! Greek yogurt or sour cream keeps them extra moist, creamed butter creates a wonderfully soft texture, and fresh blackberries add a burst of fresh flavor.

ingredients.name: (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
(50g) packed light or dark brown sugar
(120g) sour cream or plain yogurt, at room temperature
and 1/2 cups (250g) fresh or frozen blackberries (do not thaw if using frozen)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 425°F (218°C). Spray a 12-count muffin pan with nonstick spray or line with cupcake liners. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a large bowl, toss the flour, poppy seeds, baking soda, baking powder, and salt together. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: In a large bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter, sugar, and brown sugar together on high speed until smooth and creamy, about 2 minutes. Scrape down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed. Add the eggs, sour cream, vanilla extract, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Beat on medium speed for 1 minute, then turn up to high speed and beat until combined. Scrape down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed. Mixture may look a little chunky and curded; that’s ok.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: With the mixer running on low speed, add the dry ingredients and milk into the wet ingredients and beat until no flour pockets remain. Gently fold into the blackberries; they may bleed a little color and that’s ok.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Spoon the batter evenly into each cup or liner, filling each all the way to the top. Bake the muffins for 5 minutes at 425°F (218°C), then, keeping the muffins in the oven, reduce the oven temperature to 350°F (177°C). Bake for an additional 15–18 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. The total time these muffins take in the oven is about 22–23 minutes, give or take. Allow the muffins to cool for 5 minutes in the muffin pan, then transfer to a wire rack to continue cooling.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Whisk all of the icing ingredients together and drizzle over warm or cooled muffins.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Cover and store leftover muffins for up to 3 days at room temperature or up to 1 week in the refrigerator.

title: Chipotle Pumpkin Pasta
description: This easy tomato-free Chipotle Pumpkin Pasta is smoky, spicy, creamy, and the perfect way to celebrate fall flavors.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Sauté both in a skillet with the olive oil over medium heat, until the onion is soft and transparent (about 3-5 minutes).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add the chipotle powder to the onion and sauté for about one minute more. Finally, add the pumpkin purée, chicken broth, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir until the ingredients are combined and smooth.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Turn the heat down to medium-low and allow the sauce to simmer for 15 minutes. During that time, cook the pasta according to the package directions, then drain in a colander.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Once the sauce has simmered for 15 minutes, turn the heat down to low and stir in the heavy cream. Add the cooked and drained pasta to the skillet, stir to combine with the sauce, and let it heat in the sauce over low for 2-3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Finally, stir in the grated Parmesan, top with a little cracked pepper, and serve.

title: Cranberry Cardamom Spice Muffins
description: Warm, soft, and fluffy cranberry cardamom spice muffins ready in 35 minutes!

ingredients.name: (8 Tbsp; 113g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
and 1/2 cups (187g) fresh or frozen cranberries (do not thaw if using frozen)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 425°F (218°C). Spray a 12-count muffin pan with nonstick spray or line with cupcake liners. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a large bowl, toss the flour, baking soda, baking powder, cardamom, cinnamon, and salt together. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: In a large bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on high speed until smooth and creamy, about 1 minute. Add the granulated sugar and beat on high until creamed, about 2 full minutes. Scrape down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed. Add the eggs, sour cream, and vanilla extract. Beat on medium speed for 1 minute, then turn up to high speed until the mixture is combined and creamy. Scrape down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Pour the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients and beat on low speed until just about combined. Add the milk and orange zest and continue to beat on low until combined. Fold in the walnuts and cranberries with a silicone spatula.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Spoon the batter evenly into each cup or liner, filling each all the way to the top. Bake the muffins for 5 minutes at 425°F (218°C) then, keeping the muffins in the oven, reduce the oven temperature to 350°F (177°C). Bake for an additional 15-18 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. The total time these muffins take in the oven is about 22-23 minutes, give or take. Allow the muffins to cool for 5 minutes in the muffin pan, then transfer to a wire rack and drizzle with icing.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Whisk the confectioners’ sugar and orange juice together. Drizzle over muffins.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Muffins stay fresh stored in the refrigerator for 1 week.

title: Cream Cheese Stuffed French Toast
description: A baked french toast in the form of a loaf with a cream cheese filling! It tastes like a cheesecake baked into a french toast!

ingredients.name: + 2 tbsp milk ((low fat or full fat))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F. Generously butter a 10cm x 21cm / 4" x 8" loaf tin

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Make Cream Cheese Filling - beat (or mix) the cream cheese, sugar and vanilla until combined. Then add the egg and lemon juice, then beat/mix until smooth, light and fluffy.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Whisk the Egg Mixture ingredients together in a large bowl.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Dip 2 slices of bread into the Egg Mixture. Because you're using fresh bread, it should soak through with egg mixture pretty quickly.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Line the base of the loaf tin with the egg dipped bread. Cut bread if necessary to fill in gaps. Repeat twice more so you have 3 layers of egg dipped bread.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Spread all the Cream Cheese filling over the bread. Then top with two more layers of egg-dipped bread.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Dot surface with butter and scatter over the sugar.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Bake for 40 minutes until golden and puffed. Check at 30 minutes - if the top is already dark golden, then cover loosely with foil and return to the oven to finish baking (this is what I did).

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Allow to rest for 15 minutes in the tin before turning out onto a cooling rack to rest for a further 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: To serve, topped with Macerated Strawberries.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Macerated Strawberries

title: Sheet Pan Pesto Chicken Dinner
description: Sheet Pan Pesto Chicken Dinner is an easy to execute, simple yet elegant, and flavorful weeknight dinner that you’ll want to add to your weekly rotation!

ingredients.name: boneless, skinless chicken breasts (about 1.3 lbs. total) ($3.89)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 425ºF. Spray or brush a thin layer of oil onto a large sheet pan.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Slice the potatoes into 1/2 to 3/4-inch pieces. Place them in a bowl, add 1 Tbsp olive oil, the oregano, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss the potatoes until they are coated in oil and seasoning, then spread them onto half of the sheet pan.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add the tomatoes to the same bowl used for the potatoes, add another tablespoon of olive oil and another pinch of salt and pepper. Toss the tomatoes until they are coated and spread them out on the other half of the sheet pan.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Roast the potatoes and tomatoes for 20 minutes. While the potatoes and tomatoes are roasting, prepare the chicken. Place the chicken breasts on a cutting board and cover with a sheet of plastic wrap. Use a rolling pin or mallet to pound the chicken until it is an even thickness, about 1/2-inch thick. Slice each chicken breast into two pieces. Place the chicken in the oiled bowl used for the potatoes and tomatoes, add another tablespoon of olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss the chicken until it is coated, then set aside.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: After roasting for 20 minutes, stir the potatoes and tomatoes, keeping them separate, and then push each to the side slightly, opening the center of the sheet pan for the chicken. Place the chicken on the sheet pan, then return the pan to the oven and roast everything for an additional 20 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: After 20 minutes, use a meat thermometer to make sure your largest piece of chicken has reached 165ºF at its thickest point.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Spread 1 Tbsp pesto over top of each piece of chicken. Slice the mozzarella into thick slices, then place one over each piece of chicken. Change the setting on the oven from bake to broil and return the sheet pan to the oven. Let the chicken broil for 3-5 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and slightly browned. Watch the chicken closely as it broils because it will cook very quickly under the broiler.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Toss the spring mix with the dressing, then divide between four plates. Add one piece of pesto chicken to each plate, then top the chicken with a few of the roasted tomatoes. Divide the roasted potatoes between the four plates, then serve.

title: Red Velvet Cake Roll
description: This homemade red velvet cake roll filled with tangy cream cheese frosting is perfectly moist, soft, and delicious!

ingredients.name: minus 1 Tablespoon (118g) all-purpose flour (spooned & leveled), see note*
white vinegar (helps the red color stand out)
(170g) full-fat brick cream cheese, softened to room temperature
(4 Tbsp; 56g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Read the instructions through before beginning. Make sure you are prepared for step 7 immediately when that cake comes out!

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Preheat oven to 350°F (177°C). Spray a 10×15-inch baking pan with nonstick spray or grease with butter, so the parchment paper sticks. Then line it with parchment paper so the cake seamlessly releases in step 5. Spray or grease the parchment paper too. We want an extremely nonstick surface for this cake roll.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Sift the flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt together. (Make sure they’re sifted well!) Set aside.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Using a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with whisk attachment, beat the eggs for 5 minutes on high speed. They will be light and very fluffy. On medium speed, beat in the granulated sugar, brown sugar, oil, buttermilk, vinegar, food coloring, and vanilla until combined. Stop the mixer, pour in the sifted dry ingredients, then beat on low until the batter is completely combined. It will be a ruby red color.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Spread batter evenly into prepared pan. Give the pan a shake to make sure the batter is level and reaches the corners. Bake for 17 minutes or until the cake springs back when you poke it with a finger.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Meanwhile, lay a thin kitchen towel flat on the counter. Sprinkle with 1 cup of confectioners’ sugar. Once the cake comes out of the oven, immediately invert it onto the towel. Peel off the parchment paper then, starting with the narrow end, begin rolling the cake up with the towel. Do this slowly and gently. The cake will be warm as it just came out of the oven.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Allow the cake to cool completely rolled up in the towel. I stick mine in the refrigerator for about 2 hours to speed it up.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Remove the cake roll from the refrigerator and allow to sit on the counter for a few minutes to warm up as you prepare the frosting.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: In a large bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat the cream cheese for 1 minute on high speed until completely smooth and creamy. Beat in the butter until combined. Add the confectioners’ sugar and vanilla and beat on medium-high speed until combined and creamy.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Gently and slowly unroll the cake. Flatten it out and spread frosting evenly on top, leaving about a 1/2 inch border around the cake. Gently and slowly roll the cake back up, without the towel this time. Make sure you’re rolling it tightly. Some frosting may spill out the sides, that’s ok!

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Loosely cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 20 minutes (and up to 1 day, covered) before slicing and serving. Dust with more confectioners’ sugar, if desired.

title: Sriracha Nooch Popcorn
description: Sriracha Nooch Popcorn is the perfect salty snack with tons of crunch, a spicy buttery coating, and a slightly cheesy flavor thanks to nutritional yeast.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Add the cooking oil to a medium sauce pot along with one popcorn kernel. Place a lid on the pot and then place the pot over medium-high heat. Allow the oil to heat until the kernel pops.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Once the single kernel pops, lift the lid on the pot, add the rest of the popcorn kernels, and replace the lid. Give the pot a vigorous shake back and forth to make sure all the kernels are coated in the hot oil. Let the pot continue to heat over medium-high, shaking the pot occasionally, until all the kernels are popped (or until you hear the popping slow to one kernel every 3 seconds or so).

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Remove the pot from the heat and immediately pour the popped corn into a large bowl. Let the pot sit for a couple of minutes to cool slightly so the butter doesn't burn. Once slightly cooled, add the butter and sriracha to the pot. Allow the butter to melt then swirl it with the sriracha to combine.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Drizzle the sriracha butter over the popcorn in the bowl, then pour the popcorn back into the pot. Place the lid on top and give the popcorn a really good shake until all the kernels are evenly coated in sriracha butter. Return the coated popcorn to the bowl, sprinkle the nutritional yeast over top, then serve. Add salt if desired (I did not).

title: Bacon French Toast
description: A cross between a muffin, pull apart bread and french toast! Great food on the go that's perfect for feeding an army. You can even freeze them!

ingredients.name: of bread (, cut into 2cm/1" cubes (fresh or stale - see notes 1 and 2))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Combine the Egg Mixture ingredients in a bowl and whisk with a fork until combined.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add bread cubes and mix well to each piece of bread is well coated with the Egg Mixture.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: If using fresh bread, set aside for 10 to 30 minutes in the fridge. If using stale bread, set aside for 20 minutes to overnight. If you're in a rush, use your hands to gently squeeze the cubes to help them absorb the egg mixture faster.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Preheat oven to 180C/350F.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Spray a medium fry pan with oil spray and heat on high heat. Add the bacon and cook until just starting to brown. Don't cook until it is very brown because otherwise it will overcook in the oven. Remove bacon from fry pan and drain on absorbent paper.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Spray 6 holes in a muffin tin.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Mix the bread-egg mixture to evenly disperse the egg mixture that will have settled at the bottom of the bowl.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Divide 1/3 of the bread-egg mixture between the 6 muffin cups.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Sprinkle with half the bacon, then press the mixture down with medium pressure using the back of a spoon.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Top with the remaining bread mixture. Use your fingers to lightly compress them. Each muffin should be slightly mounded even after lightly compressing them. The bread cubes on top will puff back up when you bake it.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Sprinkle with remaining bacon.

steps.title: Step 12
steps.description: Bake for 15 to 20 minutes until the top is golden brown.

steps.title: Step 13
steps.description: Let rest for 5 minutes before removing from the muffin tin.

steps.title: Step 14
steps.description: Serve with maple syrup, if using.

title: Jam Jelly Doughnut French Toast
description: French toast dressed up as a strawberry jam jelly doughnut! At 230 calories per serving it's so much healthier than Krispy Creme. And remember, it's french toast, so you can have dessert for breakfast!

ingredients.name: white sandwich bread (, stale, 1.5cm/0.6" thick (see notes))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Optional: Cut the bread into rounds, as large as you can. See notes for tip.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Spread 1 tbsp of strawberry jam into the middle of 3 of the rounds, leaving an edge of about 1cm / 1/4" so when you put the sandwich together, the jam does not ooze out of the side. (See photo below) Put sandwich together.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Place egg and milk in a small bowl and beat lightly.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Spread sugar on a small plate.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Heat butter in large non stick pan over medium high heat.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Quickly dip each side of a sandwich into the egg mixture, then roll the edge in the egg mixture as well. Then place in the pan. Repeat with remaining sandwiches.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Cook each side for around 2 minutes, until a deep golden brown.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Remove from the pan and immediately place on the plate with the sugar to coat each side and the edges. The faster you get it from the pan into the sugar, the better the sugar will stick.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Hoisin Tofu Lettuce Cups
description: Skip the meat and make these light and fresh Hoisin Tofu Lettuce Cups for a fast and easy vegetarian weeknight dinner.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Remove your tofu from the package and place it between two plates or rimmed baking sheets. Place something heavy on top, like a cast iron skillet, to weigh it down and press out the excess liquid. Let the tofu press for about 30 minutes. Pour off the liquid and pat the tofu dry with a paper towel.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Roughly chop the block of tofu into small, irregular shapes. Place the chopped tofu in a bowl and sprinkle with salt and 1 Tbsp cornstarch. Fold the tofu and cornstarch together until coated, then add a second tablespoon of cornstarch and fold again.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Heat the cooking oil in a large non-stick skillet (cast iron or teflon, your choice) over medium. Once hot, add the cornstarch coated tofu. Allow the tofu to cook until golden brown on the bottom, then stir and let cook until golden brown on bottom again. Repeat this process until most of the tofu is golden and crispy on the edges (about 10 minutes total), then remove it from the heat.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add the hoisin sauce to the fried tofu and stir to coat. Slice the green onion and shred the carrot. Roughly chop the peanuts.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: To build the lettuce cups, spoon 1-2 Tbsp cooked rice into each lettuce leaf, top with 1-2 Tbsp of the hoisin tofu, then sliced green onion, shredded carrot, and chopped peanuts. Enjoy!

title: Crumb Cake Donuts
description: Baked and layered crumb cake donuts are a special treat to make for breakfast. Buttery and moist, these cake donuts are comfort food at its finest!

ingredients.name: (67g) packed light or dark brown sugar
(4 Tbsp; 56g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
(120g) plain yogurt or sour cream, at room temperature

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 350°F (177°C). Spray donut pan with non-stick spray. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Mix the brown sugar, granulated sugar, and cinnamon together in a medium bowl. Stir in the melted butter using a fork, then add the flour. Mix and crumble with a fork. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Whisk the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg together in a large bowl. Set aside. Whisk the melted butter, eggs, brown sugar, milk, yogurt, and vanilla together until completely combined. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Do not overmix. The batter will be very thick.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Spoon the batter into the donut cavities—I highly recommend using a large zipped-top bag for ease. Cut a corner off the bottom of the bag and pipe the batter into each donut cup, filling about halfway. Grab a handful of crumb topping and press down onto the batter of each donut. Pressing it snug into the batter helps prevent the crumb coating from falling off the donuts. (If you only have 1 donut pan, keep the remaining batter in the bowl at room temperature until you can bake the next batch.)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Bake for 10-11 minutes or until the edges and tops are lightly browned. Allow to cool for about two minutes then transfer to a wire rack set on a large piece of parchment paper. Bake the remaining donut batter and once baked, transfer to the wire rack. Dust the tops of each with a light coating of confectioners’ sugar, if desired.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Donuts are best served immediately. Leftovers keep well covered tightly at room temperature or in the refrigerator for 2 days.

title: Slow Cooker Vegetarian Lentil Chili
description: This Slow Cooker Vegetarian Lentil Chili makes a huge batch, is packed with flavor and nutrients, and can be made for only about 5 dollars!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Add the onion and garlic to the slow cooker along with all the other ingredients and stir to combine.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Place a lid on the slow cooker and cook on high for four hours (or low for 8 hours). After cooking, the lentils should be tender. Taste the chili and add salt to taste, if needed (this will depend on the salt content of your broth).

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Serve the chili hot, with your favorite toppings.

title: Crispy Couscous Pancake with Tomato & Onion
description: A fabulous way to prepare couscous - everyone loves the crunchy burnt bits!

ingredients.name: vegetable stock powder (or chicken stock powder, or just salt)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Combine couscous, stock powder (or salt) and water. Cover with cling wrap and put aside for 10 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a pan about 20cm wide over medium heat.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add the onion and sauté until translucent and soft, but not browned - about 3 to 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add tomato paste and sugar and stir for 1 minute.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add the diced tomatoes, salt and black pepper and cook for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Combine the tomato mixture and couscous, and mix well.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Wipe the pan (you don't need to clean it thoroughly), then heat the remaining 2tbsp olive oil over medium heat.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Add the couscous mixture, using the back of a spoon to press the mixture firmly and evenly into the pan. The firmer you press it in, the better it will hold together.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Cover (even with a lid for a larger pot is fine) and leave for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Remove from heat, loosen the edges slightly with a knife, put a plate on top, say a prayer, then flip quickly to invert the couscous pancake onto a plate with the crispy side up.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Serve warm while it is nice and crispy!

title: Greek Yogurt Apple Blueberry Bread
description: Greek yogurt apple blueberry bread with zero refined sugar! Made with honey, apples, blueberries, and Greek yogurt, this quick bread is a simple healthy snack or breakfast!

ingredients.name: (80ml) canola, vegetable, or melted coconut oil
(140g) fresh or frozen blueberries (do not thaw)

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat the oven to 350°F (177°C) and grease a 9×5-inch loaf pan.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Whisk the oil, honey, egg, yogurt, vanilla, and apple together in a medium bowl until combined. In a large bowl, whisk the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, and blueberries together. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix with a large wooden spoon or rubber spatula until combined. Avoid overmixing.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Spread batter into the prepared loaf pan. Bake for 40-50 minutes. Baking times vary, so keep an eye on yours. The bread is done when a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. If you find the top of the bread is browning too quickly in the oven, loosely cover it with aluminum foil.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Remove the bread from the oven and set on a wire rack. Allow to cool completely before slicing and serving.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Cover and store leftover bread at room temperature for up to 5 days.

title: Fresh Tomato Basil Pasta with Ricotta
description: It only takes a few fresh ingredients make this Fresh Tomato Basil Pasta with Ricotta, the perfect light lunch or dinner for summer!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Bring a large pot of water to a boil for the pasta. Once boiling, add the pasta and continue to boil until the pasta is tender. Reserve about one cup of the starchy pasta water, then drain the pasta in a colander.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: While the pasta is cooking, prepare the rest of the recipe. Slice the grape tomatoes in half and slice or roughly chop the basil. Mince the garlic.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add the olive oil to a large skillet and place it over medium heat. Once hot, add the garlic and sauté for just one minute to take the raw edge off the garlic.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add the grape tomatoes and a pinch of salt to the skillet. Stir to combine, then place a lid on the skillet and let the tomato simmer for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Let the tomatoes simmer just until they begin to break down and they have released their juices. The tomatoes should just be beginning to lose their shape and their skins should appear a bit wrinkled.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Turn the heat off and add the butter, most of the basil (save some to garnish with), and some freshly cracked pepper. Stir until the butter is melted, then taste and adjust the salt and pepper as needed. You want it to be slightly on the salty side, as the saltiness will dilute a bit once it's combined with the pasta.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Add the cooked and drained pasta and stir to coat the pasta in the tomato sauce. Add a splash or two of the reserved pasta water if the mixture appears dry.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Top the pasta with dollops of ricotta, a little more pepper, and any reserved basil, then serve!

title: Mexican Breakfast Tacos (Chorizo and Egg)
description: A great recovery breakfast that has a zing of spice and the strong flavours of Chorizo piled into a soft taco. I usually find that one is enough per person but if you're really starving, then you'll need two!

ingredients.name: / 200g Mexican or raw Spanish chorizo (, removed from casings (Note 1))
birds eye chilies (, deseeded and finely chopped (adjust to taste))
large eggs (, lightly whisked (or 6 small eggs))
small tortillas (, corn or flour (around 6" / 12 cm rounds))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Combine Pico de gallo ingredients EXCEPT THE SALT in a bowl and set aside while you prepare the rest of the breakfast.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Cook Chorizo: Heat olive oil in a non stick fry pan over high heat. Add the chorizo and cook, breaking up the pieces, until browned. Remove chorizo into a bowl. Drain excess fat but leave enough to cook the eggs.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Make the Eggs: Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic, chilies and onion to the pan and sauté for 2 to 3 minutes until translucent and starting to turn golden. Add the eggs and milk and stir slowly with a wooden spoon until the eggs start to set. Remove the pan from the stove while they are still a bit wet because they will keep cooking. Stir through salt and pepper - adjust to taste.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Warm tortillas in the microwave or oven, per packet instructions.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Add salt to pico de gallo and toss.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Assemble: Place some scrambled eggs in a tortilla then top with chorizo then pico de gallo. Garnish with cotija / feta, avocado and coriander/cilantro leaves.

title: Homemade Berry Fritters
description: This sweet summery fried fritter combines a soft yeasted dough, blueberries, and a creamy strawberry glaze made from real strawberries.

ingredients.name: Platinum Yeast from Red Star instant yeast*
(500g) all-purpose flour (spooned & leveled), plus more as needed
and 1/2 cups (225g) fresh mixed berries* (see note)
(60ml) heavy cream, half-and-half, or whole milk

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Whisk the warm milk, yeast, and 1 Tablespoon of sugar together in the bowl of your stand mixer fitted with a dough hook or paddle attachment. Cover and allow to sit for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add the remaining 7 Tablespoons of sugar, eggs, butter, vanilla extract, salt, nutmeg, and 1 cup flour. Beat on low speed for 30 seconds, scrape down the sides of the bowl with a silicone spatula, then add the remaining flour. Beat on medium speed until the dough comes together and pulls away from the sides of the bowl, about 2 minutes. If needed, add more flour, 1 Tablespoon at a time, until the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl. Don’t add too much flour, though. You want a slightly sticky dough. *If you do not own a mixer, you can mix this dough with a large wooden spoon or silicone spatula. It will take a bit of arm muscle!*

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Keep the dough in the mixer and beat for an additional 5-7 full minutes, or knead by hand on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 full minutes. (If you’re new to bread-baking, my How to Knead Dough video tutorial can help here.) If the dough becomes too sticky during the kneading process, sprinkle 1 teaspoon of flour at a time on the dough or on the work surface/in the bowl to make a soft, slightly tacky dough. Do not add more flour than you need because you do not want a dry dough. After kneading, the dough should still feel a little soft. Poke it with your finger—if it slowly bounces back, your dough is ready to rise. You can also do a “windowpane test” to see if your dough has been kneaded long enough: tear off a small (roughly golfball-size) piece of dough and gently stretch it out until it’s thin enough for light to pass through it. Hold it up to a window or light. Does light pass through the stretched dough without the dough tearing first? If so, your dough has been kneaded long enough and is ready to rise. If not, keep kneading until it passes the windowpane test.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Lightly grease a large bowl with oil or nonstick spray. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it to coat all sides in the oil. Cover the bowl with aluminum foil, plastic wrap, or a clean kitchen towel. Allow the dough to rise in a relatively warm environment for 1-2 hours or until double in size. (I always let it rise on the counter. Takes about 2 hours. For a tiny reduction in rise time, see my answer to Where Should Dough Rise? in my Baking with Yeast Guide.)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: When the dough is ready, punch it down to release the air. Turn it out onto a lightly floured work surface and with lightly floured hands, flatten the dough into a long oval about 1/2 inch thick. You can use a rolling pin if needed, but your hands should be able to stretch it out just fine. Spread blueberries onto half of the dough, as pictured above. Fold the dough over the blueberries, then seal the edges. Using your hands, work the dough into an even log, making sure the edges are totally sealed. Using a sharp knife or bench scraper, cut the dough into about 12 even strips, then cut 4-5 perpendicular strips to make a cross pattern of sorts. This is NOT an exact process, so don’t stress. You really just need a bunch of small pieces of blueberry-filled dough! Divide the cut-up dough into 12 even pieces. Mold each into a round shape and try to “enclose” the blueberries inside the dough as much as you can. (They’ll fall out of the fritters otherwise.) Place the shaped fritters on a lined baking sheet, then cover and allow to rest as you heat the oil. They will rise a bit as they rest.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Place a cooling rack on a large baking sheet. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Pour oil into a large heavy-bottomed pot set over medium heat. Using a candy/oil thermometer, heat the oil to 350°F (177°C). This will take several minutes. Add 2 fritters at a time and fry for 1-2 minutes on each side or until golden brown. Wear kitchen gloves if oil is splashing. Carefully remove with a metal spatula or metal slotted spoon. Place fritters onto prepared rack. Repeat with remaining fritters, then turn off the heat.* Some blueberries may come loose in the oil. Discard those since they’ll taste burnt.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Let the fritters cool for a few minutes as you prepare the glaze.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Place confectioners’ sugar in a medium bowl. Set aside. Purée the strawberries in a food processor. Scrape down the sides if needed. If they aren’t pureeing into a liquid, add 1 Tablespoon of the heavy cream, half-and-half, or milk you are using in the glaze. Once liquefied, pour over confectioners’ sugar, then add the cream/milk, and vanilla extract. Whisk until smooth. If needed, add more cream/milk to thin out or more confectioners’ sugar to thicken.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Dunk each fritter into glaze. Place back onto prepared rack for excess glaze to drip down. The glaze will eventually set on the fritters after about 20-30 minutes.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Fritters are best enjoyed the same day. Cover and store leftovers at room temperature for 1 day or in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat in the microwave.

title: Red Velvet Cookies
description: These cookies have a "cake like" texture so they honestly taste just like a red velvet cupcake, except they're in cookie form! Which means a) a higher frosting to "cupcake" ratio; and b) less calories per serving.

ingredients.name: sour cream or plain yogurt ((full fat))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Cookies

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Cream Cheese Frosting

title: Chocolate Cherry Almond Snack Bars
description: Chewy, crunchy, satisfying chocolate cherry almond bars made with real, wholesome ingredients.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 300°F (149°C). Line an 8-inch or 9-inch square baking pan with parchment paper with enough overhang on the sides to easily remove the bars from the pan. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Using a silicone spatula or wooden spoon, mix the honey, vanilla, almond meal, salt, and almond butter together until combined. Fold in the almonds, cherries, and chocolate chips until combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Transfer mixture to prepared baking pan and press very firmly into an even layer. You really want it packed in tight—as tight as possible. Bake for 20 minutes. Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely in the pan set on a wire rack for 1 hour, then transfer to the refrigerator to chill for 1 more hour. This helps firm up the bars which makes them stay compact. Remove bars from the pan using the overhang on the sides and cut into bars. Individually wrap each bar in plastic wrap or parchment. Store at room temperature for 1 week or in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. I find they get a little sticky at room temperature over a few days, so I prefer the refrigerator.

title: Peanut Butter Fluff Cups
description: Peanut Butter Fluff Cups are the easiest and most decadent dessert you’ve ever made. They come together in minutes with no baking required!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Make the vanilla whipped cream first by placing the heavy whipping cream, granulated sugar, and vanilla extract in a bowl. Use a whisk or a mixer to beat the cream until it forms soft peaks (when you lift the whisk it form a peak that droops over to the side). Set the whipped cream aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: In a separate bowl, whip together the peanut butter, cream cheese, powdered sugar, and milk until it is light and creamy. A hand mixer or bench mixer is suggested for this, since the mixture will be quite thick. Start on low power to prevent the powdered sugar from spraying, and increase to medium once the powder sugar has incorporated.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Add 1/3 of the whipped cream to the peanut butter mixture and gently fold them together. Once incorporated, gently fold in about 1/3 more of the whipped cream, leaving the last 1/3 to top the cups. Fold the whipped cream and peanut butter mixture together until smooth and fluffy.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Place three of the four graham crackers in a zip top bag and crush with a rolling pin, or place them in a food processor and process until they are in crumbs.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Divide the graham cracker crumbs between four cups or containers, saving a small amount to sprinkle on top. Divide the peanut butter fluff between the four cups, then top with the remaining vanilla whipped cream. Sprinkle a pinch of the graham cracker crumbs on top of each, then break the last graham cracker into four pieces and place one piece in each cup.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: You can serve the cups immediately, or refrigerate until chilled, which will give you a more solid, cheesecake-like texture (they're more like mousse when freshly made).

title: Chorizo Breakfast Hash with Crispy Smashed Potatoes and Eggs
description: A loaded breakfast hash with chorizo, bacon, cherry tomatoes, crispy smashed potatoes and eggs.

ingredients.name: rashers of bacon (, or 3 strips of streaky bacon, cut into large dice)
fresh parsley (, finely chopped (optional, for garnish))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Steam, boil or microwave the potatoes until soft. (Note 1)

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Cut the potatoes in half.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Heat a skillet or fry pan over high heat.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Add the bacon (add a dash of olive oil if you have lean bacon) and fry until browned but not crisp.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Remove onto a plate with a slotted egg flip. (Note 2)

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Add the chorizo into the same fry pan and cook each side until browned, around 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Remove on the plate with the bacon. (Note 2)

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Place the potatoes, cut side down, into the fry pan. Use the egg flip or a potato masher to "smash" the potatoes down so they flatten onto the fry pan. Sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Cook the first side until golden brown - around 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. Then turn and cook the other side until just golden brown, pressing down with the egg flip - around 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. Sprinkle with salt.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Add the bacon, chorizo and cherry tomatoes into the fry pan. Toss gently.

steps.title: Step 11
steps.description: Crack the eggs into the pan. Drizzle the water down the side of the pan, then put a lid on the fry pan (Note 3). The eggs will "steam cook".

steps.title: Step 12
steps.description: Cook until the eggs are cooked to your liking. For "just set" egg white with runny yolks, it is usually around 1 1/2 minutes.

steps.title: Step 13
steps.description: Remove from the stove, sprinkle with salt and pepper. Top with sprinkle of parsley and avocado (if using).

steps.title: Step 14
steps.description: Serve with crusty bread or toast.

title: Brownie Cookie Sandwich with Peanut Butter Frosting
description: Brownie cookies sandwiched together with a fluffy peanut butter frosting! The cookie has a thin crackly surface (just like normal brownies!) and is soft and moist on the inside. Not dense and fudgey, not cakey, it lies in between.

ingredients.name: / 1/2 cup / 115g unsalted butter (, cut into chunks)
/ 300g dark chocolate chips ((or buttons, melts or block chocolate cut into chunks))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Brownie Cookie

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Peanut Butter Frosting

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Assemble

title: Make Ahead Microwave Breakfast Scrambles
description: Make Ahead Microwave Breakfast Scrambles make a fast, easy, and healthy breakfast when you’re short on time!

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Divide the ham, bell pepper, onion, and cheddar between four resealable containers*. Cut the butter into 4, 1/2 teaspoon-sized pieces, and add one to each container. Sprinkle about 1/8 teaspoon seasoning salt into each container.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Seal the containers and store them for up to 4-5 days.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: When you're ready to prepare a microwave breakfast scramble, pour the ingredients of the container into a ceramic mug, add two large eggs, and stir to combine.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Loosely cover the mug with a paper towel or another microwave-safe object and microwave on high power for 30 seconds. Stir the contents of the mug and microwave for 15 seconds more. Stir again and continue to microwave for 15 second intervals, stirring after each, until the eggs are mostly set, but still moist. Serve immediately.

title: Vanilla Almond Snack Bars
description: Chewy and wholesome snack bars that are slightly sweet 'n salty, packed with whole foods, and are made without grains or refined sugars.

ingredients.name: and 1/3 cups (187g) whole almonds, roughly chopped

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 300°F (149°C). Line an 8-inch square baking pan or 9-inch square baking pan with parchment paper with enough overhang on the sides to easily remove the bars from the pan. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Using a rubber spatula or wooden spoon, mix the honey, vanilla, almond meal, salt, and almond butter together until combined. Fold in the almonds, chopped dates, and sunflower seeds until combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Transfer mixture to prepared baking pan and press very firmly into an even layer. You really want it packed in tight—as tight as possible. Bake for 20 minutes. Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely in the pan set on a wire rack for 1 hour, then transfer to the refrigerator to chill for 1 more hour. This helps firm up the bars which makes them stay compact. Remove bars from the pan using the overhang on the sides and cut into bars. Individually wrap each bar in plastic wrap or parchment. Store at room temperature for 1 week or in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. I find they get a little sticky at room temperature over a few days, so I prefer the refrigerator.

title: Spinach Chickpea and Quinoa Salad
description: This Spinach Chickpea and Quinoa Salad is an awesome base to build meals throughout the week and it holds up extremely well in the fridge, so you can eat better with less effort.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: While your quinoa (or other grain) is cooking, prepare the vinaigrette so it has a few minutes for the flavors to blend. In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, oregano, garlic powder, salt, and some freshly cracked pepper. Set the dressing aside.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Roughly chop the spinach into smaller pieces, then place them in a large bowl. Slice the tomatoes in half, then add them to the bowl with the spinach. Rinse and drain the chickpeas, then add them to the bowl. Lastly, crumble the feta over top. Give these ingredients a brief stir until they're combined.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Allow your quinoa or grains to cool slightly, or just to the point where it's no longer steaming. Add the warm quinoa to the salad and stir to combine. The heat from the quinoa will slightly wilt the spinach.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Finally, drizzle the dressing over top and stir briefly once more. Serve the salad immediately, or refrigerate up to 4-5 days.

title: Slow Cooker Honey Teriyaki Chicken
description: This is tender and extra saucy honey teriyaki chicken, an extremely simple and versatile dish made in the slow cooker.

ingredients.name: (60ml) vinegar (either rice wine or apple cider is great)
– 2 teaspoons minced fresh ginger (or 1/2 teaspoon ground ginger)
boneless skinless chicken breasts (about 1.5 – 2 lbs total)
optional for serving: sesame seeds and chopped scallion

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Stir the cornstarch and water together in a small bowl until combined, then pour into a medium saucepan over low-medium heat. Add the brown sugar, honey, soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, and ginger. Allow to simmer while whisking occasionally. Bring to a boil and allow to boil for 1 minute, then remove from heat.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Place chopped onion and chicken into a 4 quart or larger slow cooker. (Here’s one I own and love!) Pour teriyaki sauce on top. Cook on high for 3 hours or on low for 5 hours.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Remove the chicken from the slow cooker and, using 2 forks, shred into smaller pieces. Place back into the slow cooker and stir to combine. Cook for 1 more hour on low, stirring occasionally.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Garnish with sesame seeds and scallion, if desired, and serve on top of cooked rice or quinoa or alongside steamed vegetables. Or pile onto rolls and enjoy sandwich style. Keep leftovers in a large tupperware in the refrigerator for up to a week. Reheat in the microwave.

title: Cupcake Bouquet
description: How to arrange a beautiful cupcake bouquet complete with buttercream stems and a charming buttercream bow!

ingredients.name: and 1/2 cups (340g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
(80ml) heavy cream (milk works too but the frosting won’t be as creamy)
batch baked & cooled cupcakes (vanilla cupcakes, lemon cupcakes, chocolate cupcakes, etc)*
optional for garnish: green sprinkles, white sugar pearls

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: With a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on medium speed until creamy, about 2 minutes. Add confectioners’ sugar, heavy cream, and vanilla extract. Mix on low until it begins to come together, then increase to high speed and beat for 3 full minutes. Add another Tablespoon of cream if frosting is too thick. Add a pinch of salt if frosting is too sweet. (I add 1/8 tsp salt.)

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Spoon 3/4 cup of frosting into a separate bowl. Stir in a drop of green food coloring. Add to a piping bag fitted with the 352 leaf piping tip (see note). This will be for the stems and leaves. Set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Divide remaining frosting up evenly depending how many colors you are using. Tint frosting your desired colors.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Fit a piping bag with a 1M piping tip or 2D piping tip. Add spoonfuls of frosting to the bag, alternating colors. Your piping bag will be marbled with different colors. Twist the open end shut and squeeze the frosting down to the tip. Squeeze some frosting onto a plate until you begin to see more than 1 color. (Or just pipe this onto a cupcake!)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Place the piping tip on top of the edge of a cupcake. Using steady and medium pressure, begin piping stars all around the edges, then fill in the center. For some flair, add a few white sugar pearls to the hydrangeas!

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Place the piping tip on top of the center of the cupcake. Using steady and medium pressure, pipe a tight spiral around the cupcake. Lessen some pressure as you come to the end. If the end looks a little messy, add some leaves using the 352 leaf piping tip.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Arrange cupcakes in a circular shape on a serving platter or food-safe tray. Use any extra cupcakes as decor around the bouquet or serve separately. Pipe leaves around any edges of cupcakes to fill the gaps. Pipe the stems coming out of the bottom of the bouquet. If desired, top the stems with green sprinkles. Using the 1M piping tip or 2D piping tip and any color frosting, pipe a bow on top of the stems.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Your bouquet is ready to serve! Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 4-5 days.

title: Pot Roasted Coconut Chicken
description: Chicken in a fragrant coconut broth with an intensity of flavour that belies the few ingredients this recipe uses! The broth is so good that you will want to drink it straight.

ingredients.name: coconut oil ((or substitute with olive oil))
coconut sugar ((or substitute with brown sugar))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Preheat oven to 390F/200C.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Finely mince ONE red chili (~1 tbsp). Combine chili, ginger, garlic, oil, 1/2 cup cilantro/coriander, salt and pepper in a small bowl and set aside.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Rinse and pat the chicken dry (inside and out) with paper towels. Starting from the neck end of the chicken, use a teaspoon upside down to loosen the skin from the meat across the surface of the chicken (i.e. not the legs or wings). Just go as far as you can.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Spoon the cilantro/mixture between the meat and the skin near neck. Use the spoon to spread it a bit, then to finish it off, use your finger/hands ON the skin to spread the mixture as far and evenly as you can. Doesn't need to be perfect! :)

steps.title: Step 5
steps.description: Place the chicken stock/broth, kaffir lime leaves, lemongrass and the whole red chili into a dutch oven or baking tray. (Note 4)

steps.title: Step 6
steps.description: Place the chicken in the broth, cover with a lid and roast for 45 minutes. Remove the lid, add the coconut milk and potatoes then roast for a further 30 minutes, or until juices run clear when pierced.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.description: Remove the chicken from the broth and cover loosely with foil to rest for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.description: Skim excess fat off the surface of the broth - I usually get about 5 tbsp of fat off.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.description: Add the juice from the lime, sugar and fish sauce. Stir until the sugar is dissolved. Add remaining cilantro/coriander.

steps.title: Step 10
steps.description: Serve the chicken with the broth in a pouring jug, or cut up the chicken and serve it in the broth.

title: Beef and Cabbage Soup
description: Light, but filling, this Beef and Cabbage Soup will fill you up without weighing you down, and will keep you warm from the inside out.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Finely dice the onion and mince the garlic. Add both to a large soup pot with the olive oil and sauté over medium heat until the onions are soft (about 5 minutes). Add the ground beef and continue to sauté until browned. If using a higher fat content beef, drain the fat once the beef has browned.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Add the shredded cabbage, stewed tomatoes (with juices), oregano, basil, some freshly cracked pepper (I like it peppery, so about 20 cranks of a pepper mill), the beef broth and Worcestershire sauce. Stir everything to combine.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Place a lid on the pot, turn the heat up to high, and let it come to a boil. Once boiling, turn the heat down to low and allow it to simmer for about 30 minutes, or until the cabbage is very soft.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Taste the soup and add salt if needed (I did not add salt, but you may need to depending on the type of broth you use). Use a spoon to break any larger pieces of tomato into smaller pieces. Squeeze about 1 Tbsp of lemon juice into the soup and add a handful of chopped fresh parsley. Stir to combine, taste once more and make adjustments if needed, then serve.

title: Creamy Healthy Broccoli Soup
description: Imported from recipetineats.com.

ingredients.name: potato (, peeled and roughly chopped into cubes about 1.5cm / 1/2" cubes)
white or brown onion (, roughly diced)
/ 250 ml milk ((low fat, cow or soy))

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Cut / break broccoli into florets into pieces about half the size of a golf ball. Roughly cut most of the stem, other than the main stem, into thumb size pieces. Discard the main stem, or keep for another use.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Place all ingredients except milk, salt and pepper in pot, put on lid, bring to boil then turn down heat to medium and let it rapidly simmer for 8 to 10 minutes.

steps.title: Step 3
